program.synopsis: In 1889 in Oklahoma Territory, the land rush attracts thousands, including many outlaws. A gang led by Wild Bill Doolan includes the Sundance Kid and the Younger brothers, who intend to take advantage of the confusion to rob banks.
Pacer Burton is the mixed-race son of a white rancher and his second wife, a Kiowa Indian. As struggle starts between the whites and the Kiowas, the Burton family is split between loyalties.
A goat and biscuits save a family from Indians.
Having survived his own hanging, hired killer James Devlin turns over a new leaf, defending a widow and her son from a greedy land baron.
In 1836 Davy Crockett, Jim Bowie and more than 180 Texans hold off the Mexican army for 13 days during Sam Houston's revolution.
A gunslinger rides into North Fork for a meeting with Lucas.
Lou looks for a way out when the man her dying father promised her to arrives.
The Lone Ranger joins a band of thieves, hoping to trap them.
A man confesses in an effort to prevent his brother from hanging for a crime he didn't commit.
Sheriff Hollister decides to go undercover to prevent editor Claibourne from being murdered by a hitman who has been hired by a vengeful, local rancher whom the editor once helped to send to prison.

As Clay watches the arrival of the stage from Tucson, a shooting down the street diverts his attention, and a $30,000 army payroll is stolen from a cavalry captain on the stage. Wounded and disarmed, he's forced into a battle to solve the crime.
A Mormon leader is determined to enforce God's law - with a gun.
A physician courts disaster by aiding one of two feuding Indians.
Ben Harper, a stranger passing through a frontier town, becomes the target of the sheriff, Adam Prescott, who believes Ben is the doctor who amputated his leg during the Civil War.
Gunslinger, Tod Owen, is placed on trial for murder, but before the jury can return its verdict, Owen escapes.

After Longley is forced to kill a barfly that tried to shoot him in the back, he learns a quirk in the Montana law code - any man who slays another in a fair fight is responsible for the care and feeding of the widow and children until she gets married. Longley's efforts to escape the snare all come to naught because the widow is in love with him and wants him for a husband until he resorts to reverse psychology.
While escorting to prison the leader of a notorious gang, a sheriff and a deputy are attacked by the gang.

Hunting guide Bat Masterson takes on a party bent on hitting bear. As the expedition moves forward, Bat realizes that three of his companions are fugitives from murder and robbery charges in other states.

Joe Quince tries running Jan and Woody Larkin out of town. Joe wants the store they inherited from their father, but they won't sell. In the midst of this danger, Bat Masterson agrees to teach Woody how to handle a gun.
Three soldiers falsely convicted of murder find out they are scapegoats for their commanding officer's unprovoked attack on Indians.
Agent Hardie goes undercover to thwart a Wells Fargo robbery, but his identity may be compromised.
Outlaws overturn a stage and kill the driver, leaving two survivors - a little girl and a musician.
A Confederate Major and his troops are falsely lead to believe that the Civil War has not ended and they become wanted men when they attack a Union Army wagon train.
An officer accused of cowardice volunteers to bring back General Custers's body after Little Big Horn.

In the last days of the Wild West, Calamity Jane works as a muleskinner for Custer, has a fling with Wild Bill Hickok, and tries to reclaim her daughter when she travels to London with Buffalo Bill's Wild West Show.

In the last days of the Wild West, Calamity Jane works as a muleskinner for Custer, has a fling with Wild Bill Hickok, and tries to reclaim her daughter when she travels to London with Buffalo Bill's Wild West Show.

program.episode_title: The Great Pinto Bean Gold Hunt
program.synopsis: When two down-on-their-luck miners find gold nuggets mixed in with the beans they bought in town, they decide to search for the source, hoping for more.
A besieged mother and son trust an Indian scout.
A stranger's questions about a hanged Indian make him unwelcome in a California oil town.
A former sheriff returns home from the Civil War to find everyone thought he was dead, and his sweetheart engaged to his land-grabbing buddy.
An assassination plot unfolds as four gunmen disguised as cavalrymen enter North Fork. Their target: Sen. Jim Borden. Their weapon: a Gatling gun.
Sharpshooter Marty Blair tries to persuade Lucas to buy Mark a rifle.
The Lone Ranger and Tonto help Rick Merill keep a railroad franchise.
The son of an outlaw plans to kill his father.

General amnesty follows the shooting of young Les Tanner by hired killers. Hollister cannot arrest the gunmen because of amnesty but he prevents a new flare-up of the feud by arresting the victim's brother who is out for revenge.

Young Mark Thompson shoots an old miner in the back, robs him and flees. Sheriff Hollister trails him to his mother's home in Paradise. In order to expose the son's true character to his faithful mother, Hollister must put his own life on the line.

Postal officials ask Harry, a gunman, to find a way to stop King Fisher from blocking mail from arriving in Uvalde, but Fisher fears he will lose his power if he opens his road to the post office. Dale Robertson stars as Harry.
A young man hunts for gold, hoping to impress his girl so she'll accept his proposal, but the arrival of a wealthy traveler makes him think again. Jess Ivy
Belle Garrison, owner of an Arizona freight line, tries to keep a family from muscling in on her company. She relies on an old Indian trick to scare them off.

A man sentenced to hang escapes and returns to his home town to kill the witness whose false testimony convicted him. Hunted by a posse and forced into hiding, the fugitive enlists the aid of his girlfriend and his business partner to track down the witness.

While riding down a trail, Bill is accosted by a 'boy' who demands he help 'his' father, who has been shot in the stomach. Before the man dies, he asks Bill to take care of his daughter and Bill discovers that the mud-splattered youth who accosted him is really a pretty girl with her long tresses tucked under her cap. Before the man can be a buried, a posse rides into their camp and the express agent accuses the dead man of robbery, but none of the stolen money can be found in the dead man's saddlebags. Henrietta asks Bill to help her clear her father's name.

Bill has been hired to guide two Easterners who want to capture wild stallions to use as studs for breeding. Bill and the Dowds get off on the wrong foot when the husband becomes jealous of his old friendship with his wife. Dowd hires three gunslingers to fake a robbery so that he drive them off and look like a hero in his wife's eyes but the owlhoots plan to steal all of the Easterners money and kill Bill Longley in the bargain.
Influential John Conant calls Bat Masterson to Cheyenne. The most adept card player of the frontier, Bat is able to test the rumors that Steve Haley - Conant's daughter's fiancé - is a poker cheat.
Prospector Bat Masterson carries three thirds of a treasure map on an expedition, when he seeks a reputedly fabulous cache. Each part of the map belongs to one of the mutually distrustful heirs.
Hardie shows a female writer from New England that the Wild West is not as it seems.
A rogue bounty hunter kills a robber, steals his loot and refuses to hand it over to Agent Hardie.
Hardie is summoned to help an old friend whose husband committed a robbery and escaped jail.
A Texan is heading home with enough money to start his own ranch, but when he stops in a crooked town, he's robbed and framed for murder.
After their service in the Civil War, four brothers go their separate ways, but later find themselves on opposite sides of a final showdown.

Infamous outlaw, Ben Wade, is captured by the law and rancher Dan Evans finds himself part of the posse sworn in to escort Wade to the 3:10 to Yuma train that will transport him to prison and a certain death sentence.
A young woman flirts with a ranch hand while waiting for a proposal from her beau despite her grandmother's advice, only to learn the hard way that granny was absolutely right.
Wagon master Caleb must bring his wayward son, Johnny, to justice after the boy goes too far.
After a gold prospector is killed by masked robbers, a detective is hired to find the surviving killer as well as the prospector's legal inheritors.
A U.S. tycoon hires an 1880s gunfighter to find his brother in Mexico.
Fast-draw artist Wade Randall is less than anxious to display his prowess.
A speculator will stop at nothing to grab Lucas's land.
A reporter, believing the Lone Ranger is a fraud, attempts to expose him.
Outlaws threaten a publisher's right to free speech.
As silver strikes increase, more miners are robbed and murdered, forcing Hollister to hunt down the killers; a stolen black diamond turns out to be a clue in the crimes.

When Chris Anderson confesses to Sheriff Hollister that Brewster Flat's Sheriff Binns and his deputy were in on a robbery, Sheriff Hollister cuts himself into the deal to force a showdown among the thieves and expose a double cross.
L. Frank Baum struggles with his newspaper business, preferring to tell fantasy stories to local children about colorful characters from a land called Oz.

program.episode_title: The Biggest Little Post Office In The World
program.synopsis: Dale Robertson plays a postmaster who wheels and deals in stamps.

A man who has spent most of his life working other people's property vows to own land of his own. He homesteads on government land until violence breaks out with ranchers who want the land for grazing areas. The man refuses to move and fences in his land with barbed wire.
Frontier sheriff, Tom Baker, swears in his younger brother, Lane, as his deputy, but soon learns that his brother's idea of law enforcement is to kill any lawbreaker, regardless of the crime.

Bill Longley shares a stagecoach compartment with Jody Sammett and his pregnant wife Maria on their journey to meet Jody's father, Big Jim Sammett for Christmas. Jody dies helping to fight off bandits who try to rob their stagecoach and Maria's father-in-law refuses to take in his son's Mexican-American wife or allow anyone in town to help her even though she has started in labor. While Bill tries to convince the old man to change his mind, the unsuccessful bandits try to abduct the town's only doctor to operate on their seriously wounded companion.

After Longley rescues the wounded Reverend Kilgore from two would-be bushwhackers, he learns that the parson was heading for Phillipsburg, a town with a reputation for killing men of the cloth. Bill decides to pose as the minister to investigate, but without his holster at the minister's insistence. With only Kilgore's Bible for protection, Bill attempts to rally the townspeople against the town boss and his crooked judge.

Bat Masterson mixes in politics when President Rutherford B. Bayes and General William Tecumseh Sherman, the scourge of Atlanta, come to Dodge City on a campaign tour. Bat goes into action when unrest threatens because there are rumors that ex-Confederates threaten violence to Sherman.

Between the scorching desert and ruthless trail pirates, wagon train after wagon train is destroyed. The unique method the pirates use to lure the wagons astray, puts Bat Masterson on the track of a monstrous conspiracy.
A valuable bracelet is stolen during a stage holdup, and a dog helps Hardie catch the jewel thief.
The owner of a large mining operation wants to contract Wells Fargo to protect her business.
Agent Hardie rides to Calico where a competitor of Wells Fargo is forcing passengers to transfer to his stage line at gunpoint.
A fellow Wells Fargo agent offers Hardie a large reward for the capture of an outlaw—dead or alive.
An outlaw tries to leave the family gang and go straight.
A straight-shooting sheriff and a newspaperwoman take on a band of outlaws in the Oklahoma panhandle.
An officer accused of cowardice volunteers to bring back General Custers's body after Little Big Horn.
A war veteran takes on an evil arthritic rancher and his murderous daughter to stop them from forcing out settlers and laying claim to their land.
Plague-ridden ranchers pinpoint a Japanese settlement as the source of trouble.
Indians reject the doctrines of Christianity in this biographical tale of missionary priest Jean Baptiste Lamy.
A Confederate Major and his troops are falsely lead to believe that the Civil War has not ended and they become wanted men when they attack a Union Army wagon train.
A con man in the West of the 1880s sets his sights on the gold strike in Cascade, Nev., but is beaten at his own game.
Mark blames himself for a friend's accidental death.

Nils Swenson, North Fork's blacksmith, volunteers to take the marshal's place for a few days. To help maintain peace, Nils institutes a policy that anyone coming into town must turn in their weapons. The plan backfires when some dishonest men are in town.
A lynch mob waits for the arrival of the stage that is reputedly carrying a notorious outlaw.
A prisoner uses a secret exit old Toby built 30 years earlier.

Convicted of murder, Sam Carver escapes the custody of Deputy Marshal Bledsoe thanks to some rowdy conventioneers aboard the train. Jumping off, he heads to Tombstone Territory where he takes refuge at the Hendrick's ranch.

Sheriff Clay Hollister rides the stage to Laramie with a court order concerning a murder indictment against a man named Rowan. Clay finds that his fellow passengers include Rowan himself under an alias and a hired gun paid to kill the Sheriff.
There's more than gold in store for prim Wilhelmina Vail when she tackles a claim in rugged Utah territory.
A cowboy and an Indian join forces to secure dowries for their sweethearts.
Dan Case arrives in the town of Bitter Creek. Though he is trying to live down his past as a gunfighter, the stranger learns that his presence has a decided effect on three of the town's inhabitants.
A man arrives in a frontier town seeking to buy a ranch. He witnesses a robbery and murder and attempts to stop the fleeing gunman.

Longley is hired to drive the first herd of cattle directly from the Texas Panhandle to Denver. When he arrives he discovers that the woman who hired him, Jenny Brewster aka The Duchess of Denver, was the daughter of a sharecropper who lived on his plantation before the Civil War and is none to happy when Bill doesn't recognize her immediately. The woman's current love interest is jealous of Longley, too. He precipitates a duel that Longley is sure to lose since it's as crooked as the roulette wheels in his casino.

A crooked sheriff and his henchmen are attempting to continue their reign of terror by running a crooked election. Longley persuades an alcoholic former Harvard law professor to stand-up to the old sheriff and then backs him when he must evict the gunsels from a ranch they're trespassing on.
The sheriff of Trinidad used Bat's name in order to get the job. When Bat hears about, it he goes to investigate.
Trail boss Bat Masterson runs afoul of an illegal toll as he leads a drive to the rail head.
Hardie suspects a casino owner is selling illegal guns to insurgent Indians.
An outlaw released early from prison has a hit-list of those responsible for his capture, including Hardie.
Hardie carries an Army payroll to Mesa City and becomes a target.
Hardie heads to Warburg to find out who killed his friend Sheriff Prescott, but he’s met with resistance from the new Sheriff in town.
Betrayed by his best friend, a cowboy must protect his herd from rustlers and risk losing his fiancee.

When notorious American gunslinger, The Montana Kid, staggers into the tiny Canadian hamlet of Barclay's Brush, life for the town's 17 inhabitants is about to get exciting. The Kid immediately gets into an unfortunate altercation with Jack, the town's surly blacksmith, which leads to the The Kid 'calling him out' for a showdown. But in a place totally ill-prepared to deal with a classic gun fight and without a single working pistol to be found, adhering to the code of the American Wild West may prove difficult.
A stagecoach stop employee and a stranded woman traveler find themselves at the mercy of four desperate outlaws intent on robbing the next day's gold shipment.
A bounty hunter escorts a killer to town to be hanged, but is allowing the man's outlaw brother to catch up with him for a showdown over a shocking previous murder.
Diego learns of a plot to sell imprisoned townspeople into the slave trade.
Diego suspects a family friend may be hiding more sinister intentions.
Diego continues his foppish ruse when Galindo calls for vigilante justice.
A mysterious stranger takes control of the territory's water supply.
A homesteader who has gold on his land is terrorized by land-grabbers.
A feuding rancher is blamed for his neighbor's murder.
A man who has sworn to murder the Lone Ranger enlists the aid of a teenage boy.
The Lone Ranger and Tonto trail a gang of desperados who have an uncanny ability to stay one step ahead.

In 1889 in Oklahoma Territory, the land rush attracts thousands, including many outlaws. A gang led by Wild Bill Doolan includes the Sundance Kid and the Younger brothers, who intend to take advantage of the confusion to rob banks.
Betrayed by his best friend, a cowboy must protect his herd from rustlers and risk losing his fiancee.
A frontier widow aims to raise sheep despite a cattle rancher, in old Wyoming.

The Sisters brothers find themselves on a journey through the Northwest, bringing them to the mountains of Oregon, a dangerous brothel in a small town and the gold rush land of California- an adventure that tests the deadly family ties that bind.

Prostitutes Cody, Anita, Eileen and, work a saloon in the Old West. They decide to flee the bordello for a better life elsewhere, but trouble follows close behind. They're tailed by Pinkerton detectives, Cody's savings are stolen by a bandit, and Eileen is wrongfully jailed for bank robbery. Now the remaining three girls must decide whether to liberate their friend or their funds first.
Two very different women become a bank-robbing duo in an effort to combat a ruthless enforcer terrorizing their Mexican town.

Infamous outlaw, Ben Wade, is captured by the law and rancher Dan Evans finds himself part of the posse sworn in to escort Wade to the 3:10 to Yuma train that will transport him to prison and a certain death sentence.

The Sisters brothers find themselves on a journey through the Northwest, bringing them to the mountains of Oregon, a dangerous brothel in a small town and the gold rush land of California- an adventure that tests the deadly family ties that bind.
Two brothers on opposite sides of the law become embroiled in an Arizona range war between cattlemen and farmers.
Desperately hoping to turn the tide of the war, a Confederate soldier steals $2,000,000 worth of Union gold bars and heads for the Mexican border, where he hopes to buy munitions and supplies.
In Colorado territory, outlaw Wes McQueen escapes jail to pull a railroad robbery but, upon meeting pretty settler Julie Ann, he wonders about going straight.
An ex-union soldier is hired to transport gold from a mining community through dangerous territory. But what he doesn't realize is that his partner and old friend is plotting to double-cross him.
After arriving in Texas to escape a scandal back east, a lawyer becomes involved in the independence of Texas.
A cowboy tries to support four orphan boys and a runaway girl, without his boss knowing it.
A woman sells her successful business in New Orleans to find the men who killed her father.
After pulling a bank heist in Mexico, an outlaw is betrayed by his partner who runs off with the loot and leaves him to end up in prison. Once free, he becomes a sheriff to enact revenge.

program.episode_title: The Man Who Planted Gold In California
program.synopsis: A sheriff schemes to win an interest in California's wine industry.
A town's pie supply is threatened when the loveless lady baker decides to leave.
An outlaw tries to leave the family gang and go straight.
Young farmboy who always wanted to be a cowhand talks a tough trail boss into hiring him on a cattle drive.
Lucas's self-confidence begins to slip away when he misses an easy shot.

A murder occurs at a railroad camp near North Fork, and a young Indian friend of Lucas' is accused of the killing. When Lucas tries to help the boy, he finds himself on trial and threatened with hanging for aiding and abetting a fugitive.
Crooks kidnap an engineer in a bid to gain control of land that is going to be sold to the railroad.
Blame falls on a young man when his estranged father is shot.

Harvey Logan and his gunfighters take over the town of Sunglow and have themselves a spree. Notified that the telegraph line to Sunglow is dead, Sheriff Hollister and editor Claibourne ride over to investigate and are trapped and thrown into jail.

Hollister hears a distant shot, but finds nothing to explain it. When Nancy Cooley arrives in Tombstone looking for her uncle who cannot be found, the sheriff solves the riddle of his disappearance and saves Nancy from becoming a gunman's victim.
Indians threaten a mission.
Apache policemen try to solve the murder of a white settler before vigilantes raid their reservation.

The famed gunman, Doc Holliday, visits Lee and Julie Brand, a young married couple who are friends of his. He learns that Lee is paying protection money to a gang of thieves in order to keep his home and ranch safe.
In a frontier town, Dr. Alan McMurray is called out to treat a man for gunshot wounds he incurred when stealing.
Longley rescues the lone survivor of a murderous stagecoach robbery, but the man refuses to help him identify the three killers.

Longley and the town's newspaper editor come to the assistance of a Hungarian peddler when he is attacked by local toughs. When the editor is murdered by the same gunsels, the peddler must choose between helping the Texan bring the killers to justice or fleeing with his pregnant wife.

At the request of General Phil Sheridan, heroic Bat Masterson challenges renegade Chief Stone Calf. Purpose of the challenge: Rescue the Rafferty sisters from Stone Claf's war party which kidnapped them.
Outlaw and idle-boaster bailey Harper says Bat Masterson will never find him. After Harper steals Bat's gun, money, and horse, Bat tests his great skill at trailing fugitives.
Stagecoach passengers are held captive by outlaws at a Wells Fargo station.
A woman desperate for money helps a convict escape for a share of his loot.
A beautiful gypsy is the only witness that can help Agent Hardie investigate and find a passenger kidnapped from a Wells Fargo stage.
On the stagecoach to Cheyenne a mixed group of passengers must work together to survive the arduous journey and the Indian attacks.
A cattle baron fights to tame both the West and his estranged wife who deserted him and returns ready to battle over their daughter's future.

After the Civil War, ranch owner Gil Hanley helps a stranger in desperate need of medical attention. Soon he is questioned by Union soldiers about the stranger, who turns out to be an ex-Confederate soldier. Hanley, also an ex-Confederate, is unjustly convicted as a conspirator and sentenced to life in a brutal desert prison in New Mexico.
An Indian fights prejudice to land an Army contract.
A gambler is determined to collect his poker winnings - half of Tombstone, Arizona.
A Texan is heading home with enough money to start his own ranch, but when he stops in a crooked town, he's robbed and framed for murder.
A railroad man and the owner of a freight line battle for control of a crucial mountain pass.

On the road Lucas and Mark come upon Winslow Quince, a peddler whose wagon has broken down, and help him get his wagon into North Fork for repair. While in North Fork Quince barters several items that implicate him in a series of murders.
Gridley Mau is gunning for Lucas, who killed his father during an attempted robbery.
A sheriff releases a prisoner to visit his critically ill mother.
Men in a far-removed outpost of the Pony Express peddle army secrets to the Indians.

When a British writer arrives in Tombstone to gather material for a series about the American West, he tags along with Sheriff Hollister and a prisoner as they travel to Yuma prison. But the journalist is devising a dangerous plot.

The great fire of 1881 nearly wiped out the town. As a result, Tombstone is in danger of losing its status as County Seat to nearby Benson, leading to a feud over the matter. Hollister's guns must right a wrong when fire mysteriously strikes again.
A mining-camp cook stirs stew with one hand - and filches gold with the other.

program.episode_title: How Death Valley Got Its Name
program.synopsis: A group of pioneers finds a desolate wasteland on their way to California, which they aptly name Death Valley.
An Easterner learns that his brother has been killed out West and sets out to find the murderer.
An outlaw is captured and is being returned to justice. When the man's captor is bitten by a rattlesnake, the outlaw must choose between leaving him to die or escaping to freedom.

Longley attempts to escort Yancey Lewis, a bank robber sentenced to spend five years in prison in a distant town. Longley not only has to deal with a howling windstorm and an empty canteen, but the outlaw's gang and his pretty girlfriend who are all trying to rescue him - not so much out of loyalty, but Yancey's the only person who knows where the loot from the heist is hidden.

Longley's old friend, pretty ranch owner Martha Driscoll, negotiates a highly successful sale at the end of a cattle drive. Although her friends urge her to put her money in the bank until she's ready to board a train home, Martha refuses and ends up being robbed and murdered - but not before naming her killer - Clint Gleason, one of her trailhands. Longley tracks the killer to his hometown where his father is sheriff and learns that the lawman implicitly believes his son is innocent of whatever crimes others accuse him of - even killing women.

he Sheriff of Mason City insists that gambler Bat Masterson join the Protective Gamblers' Association. This little group seems unsavory to Bat - he refuses and is thrown behind bars by the sheriff who controls the Association.

Bat Masterson, frequent target of gunfights, falls into the sights of Harry Varden's trigger man. Varden, biggest saloonkeeper in Dodge City, fears that Bat plans to relieve him of some property - and he figures his only recourse is to eliminate Masterson.
A Mexican girl and her American lover are terrorized by the girl's jilted fiance.
Agent Hardie is hired to deliver a bride-to-be and her fortune to Baton Rouge where she is to be married.
Hardie investigates reports of a gold hijacking in the Walking Mountain mine.
A rancher leads a posse to recover his kidnapped grandson from outlaws.

program.episode_title: Man Who Shot Liberty Valance, The
program.synopsis: An experienced gunman and a peace-loving tenderfoot clash with a Western bully.
Desperately hoping to turn the tide of the war, a Confederate soldier steals $2,000,000 worth of Union gold bars and heads for the Mexican border, where he hopes to buy munitions and supplies.
Mamie, the grocer's daughter, puts her suitors to a test: she'll marry the one who corners the local egg market.
Larry Madden returns after several years still vowing to avenge himself against the humiliating whipping he received at the hands of cattle baron Tucker Ordway.
An ex-union soldier is hired to transport gold from a mining community through dangerous territory. But what he doesn't realize is that his partner and old friend is plotting to double-cross him.
A Japanese nobleman seeks revenge when his servant is insulted.
At Halloween, rustlers take advantage of the seasonal disguises to raid a cattle ranch.
An Indian is murdered, and only the trial and execution of the killer will prevent an Indian uprising.
A mysterious stranger becomes involved in a battle between ranchers over water rights.

A rancher hires a gunfighter to protect him from Jeff Harper, last member of a family which has waged a feud with the rancher. Harper says he wants a truce with the rancher, but the hired gunfighter's trigger finger itches.

Sheriff Hollister arrests pretty Anita Torreon as a receiver of loot for a holdup gang and takes her to Prescott to stand trial. But, aboard the stagecoach, a disguised gunmen suddenly appear to rescue Anita and recover the loot.
Two people set out into the desert in search of the lost Pegleg Mine.
A quiet and retiring dressmaker for the dance hall girls in a western mining camp is discovered to be Tiger Lil, a former dance hall queen of Virginia City.

Marshal Cam Tolby faces the enmity of the townspeople when he tries to take an outlaw to Prescott, Arizona, to stand trial for robbery and murder. His difficulties increase when the outlaw, despite handcuffs, manages to save a group of schoolchildren from disaster.

A man who was once a fast gun has become a drunkard. Knowing that his friends are skeptical of his professed speed and accuracy with a gun, the old man tries to prove his skill when a desperado rides into town.

Bill Longley returns from a cattle drive and discovers an impostor has stolen his mail, destroyed a saloon and dallied with a pretty girl, causing his father to demand a shotgun wedding. The impostor, a criminal recently sprung from jail by a crooked gambler with a grudge against Longley, starts to have a change of heart when he discovers the respect and admiration others have for a man who stands up for the weak and has second thoughts about luring Longley into the trap the gambler has set for the Texan.

Longley has just ridden into the town of Yellow Jacket, when he sees a gunman unhitching a team of horses over the loud objections of a pretty woman. When Longley intervenes, he discovers the man is only carrying out an obscure town ordinance that forbids leaving a wagon on the street overnight - the fine being $50, payable to the town marshal. Longley learns that the marshal has been lining his pockets with fines for obscure town laws he enforces with vigor and determines to beat the man at his own game.

Bat Masterson is relieved of $10,000 in a daring riverboat hold up. After the clever band of robbers works its way systematically through the entire passenger list of a Missouri boat, Bat goes into action.

Bat commands one of the forces in a railroad war. Mace Pomeroy, frantically attempting to beat General Moran in laying tracks through a canyon, offers Bat a small fortune to help fight Moran. Bat, however, decides for Moran and ends up in a historic gang fight in the wilds of the canyon country.
Hardie hopes to solve a murder case by refusing to deliver a herd of cattle until a vet can certify them.

Jim Hardie is assigned to ride shotgun on a stagecoach that was held up carrying a valuable cargo of gold, but has to contend with a driver who thinks he can handle any outlaws he meets with all by himself.
Marshall Ogburn is fired after he shoots an unarmed suspect in the heat of a violent burglary of a Wells Fargo bank. Hardie is sent to investigate this case that may lead to more than money.
A cowboy fights for his father's ranch which his uncle has seized with forgery.
A saloonkeeper's partner frames him for gold robberies, with a woman caught in the middle.

An actual historical figure, the Apache Kid aided in the capture of Geronimo and his band. Later, as a renegade himself, the Kid rescues an old friend, a white Army Scout, and takes him back to the fort, risking his own freedom in an act of friendship and sacrifice.
Rumors drive a rancher to murder and get him hanged by a lynch mob; a sheriff finds their source.
The story of Aaron Winters and his wife Rosie and their discovery of borax in Death Valley.

Prostitutes Cody, Anita, Eileen and, work a saloon in the Old West. They decide to flee the bordello for a better life elsewhere, but trouble follows close behind. They're tailed by Pinkerton detectives, Cody's savings are stolen by a bandit, and Eileen is wrongfully jailed for bank robbery. Now the remaining three girls must decide whether to liberate their friend or their funds first.
Two very different women become a bank-robbing duo in an effort to combat a ruthless enforcer terrorizing their Mexican town.
A snake crawls into Lucas's bed, and Mark must lure it away before it strikes.
Lucas is thrown into jail to stop him from murdering an old enemy.
An outlaw gang has ambitions to have one of its members elected sheriff.
The Lone Ranger and Tonto counsel a young Indian boy who may be getting into trouble with the law.

Beautiful Elizabeth Blythe comes from New York to Tombstone to write a story about "the town too tough to die." Her nose for news promptly leads her into mortal danger when she is captured by outlaw Ed Chandler.

Released from prison together, Jake Conroy and Hobe Jones scheme to rob a rich mine owner. With the cash in their control, the thieves ride hard from Mexico with Sheriff Clay Hollister and Editor Harris Claibourne in pursuit.
After pursuing a young outlaw in the Death Valley region, a sheriff discovers that the boy is his brother.
A hard-rock miner and champion driller is encouraged by a dancehall girl to study law after he loses an arm in a shooting fracas.
Clay Culhane, a gunman tired of violence, hangs up his guns and tries to make a new life for himself as an attorney. But he finds that his reputation as a gunman hinders him.

Seeking shelter during a violent prairie storm, two cowboys come upon a long-forgotten ghost town. This ghost town is inhabited, however, and the inhabitants try desperately to remain hidden from the intruders.

Wylie Ames, a big gruff widower, has been corresponding with a lonely woman whose personal ad he saw in an out-of town paper. However, she is coming to meet him and this creates a big problem. He asks his old army captain, Bill Longley, to meet her and break the news that Wylie is not the attractive gentleman she is expecting. Also a man Wylie knocked out in a barroom fight dies and the family wants revenge - Longley is in for even bigger problems.

A crooked gambler with a Kentucky-born thoroughbred prods a drunken rancher into betting his entire spread and all the money he has in the bank on a horse race. The gambler tries to incapacitate the rancher's entry in the race but fails; however, what his henchmen couldn't accomplish a rattlesnake bite could and the horse comes up lame racing to fetch the doctor. Longley agrees to allow his quarter-horse to enter the race instead.

Morganville's bully-boy, Dan Morgan - to his great misfortune - cheats Bat in a poker game. Bat executes a bold plan to retrieve his money: He discovers the names of Morgan's other victims, and sets out to settle scores for them as well as for himself.

Lady newspaper editors are a great and colorful Frontier tradition. One of them, Jo Hart, inveighs against Bat Masterson, calling him a ruthless gunman and scoundrel. When Bat calls on the lady to set the record straight, she exercises her "woman's prerogative" of changing her mind.
Hardie goes undercover to the town of Alder Gulch to try to infiltrate the outlaw gang suspected of robbing Wells Fargo stagecoaches and freight wagons.
Jim is sent to Canada to identify the corpse of an outlaw, but the way the man was killed makes him hard to identify.
The son of a dead man accused of robbing a Wells Fargo stage coach is still convinced that his father has been falsely accused and hopes Jim Hardie finds the real culprits.
When a Wells Fargo office is robbed and one of Jim Hardie's friends is murdered, Hardie is intent on solving the crime.
A woman learns to shoot in order to exact vengeance on the men who raped her and killed her husband.
A stagecoach stop employee and a stranded woman traveler find themselves at the mercy of four desperate outlaws intent on robbing the next day's gold shipment.

Recently rescued from an Indian ambush, a Southern loner rides into town and wins the deed to the local saloon in a card game against its menacing card-cheat owner. With secession causing tensions to rise between Denver's Northerner majority and Southerner minority, he is pressured to support his Confederate brothers, but he remains firmly committed to his one true cause - gold!
Two brothers on opposite sides of the law become embroiled in an Arizona range war between cattlemen and farmers.
A Bostonian who has married a local girl in a desert mining town refuses to invite the townspeople into his home on his wedding night, thereby instigating a three-day chivaree.

program.episode_title: The Lady With The Blue Silk Umbrella
program.synopsis: The story of Mary Helen Crosby who, in the company of Gen. John Bidwell, custodian of the papers admitting California to the Union, concealed the papers in her umbrella to insure their safe arrival at San Francisco.
A posse of ranchers and U.S. Cavalrymen storm Montezuma's Castle to rescue a girl abducted by renegade Indians.
After arriving in Texas to escape a scandal back east, a lawyer becomes involved in the independence of Texas.
Lucas is baffled when the marshal of a neighboring town refuses to hold a gambler who just attempted murder.
Lucas begins a trek to find a cure for Mark, who is paralyzed after a bad fall.
The Lone Ranger almost becomes a victim of a bomb that's being used to catch a robber.
A retail-store owner faces violent opposition from a competitor.

Hollister stops gunman Jess Buckhorn from shooting Little Hawk who denies stealing horses from a mission. Attorney Fred Griffin, the gunman's secret employer, is plotting to discredit the Indians so he can gain title to their land.

Writer Jay Pell sets out to create a new legendary bad man for his readers. He persuades an outlaw to hold up a stagecoach so that he can write an eyewitness account. When things go wrong, Sheriff Clay Hollister is tricked into another scheme.
A young widow from the East offers serious competition to the proprietor of a Nevada cafe when she opens a rival restaurant and discovers gold on property that she purchased from him.
A young man who was reared as an Indian after the death of his parents in Death Valley is obliged to prove his identity in order to marry a white girl whom he loves.

Julie Holman swears vengeance when her husband is murdered. Suspecting Carl Benson, a man with whom her husband had quarreled, Julie rides out to his ranch. She finds that Benson has disappeared and sets out to track him down.

An embittered man who is hated by the townspeople, shoots and kills one of the town's leading citizens. Sheriff Ross Conners insists that the man be brought to trial, but the townspeople prepare for a lynching.

Longley rides to the rescue when four gunmen bushwhack a man riding in a buggy. Longley takes the man to a nearby ranch house, but neither the owner or the town doctor wishes to assist the wounded man, a judge scheduled to preside over the trial of the local land baron. When Longley reports the shooting to the sheriff, the lawman can't find any evidence to support the Texan's version of events - or the judge himself for that matter.

Longley, trapped by a landslide, is rescued by Johnny Hinshaw who the Texan soon discovers is on the run from the law. Johnny's accused of shooting a man after a quarrel and since he's already served a jail term and has two brothers on the run from the law he seems like a reasonable suspect to the sheriff - the more so since Johnny's sweet on the lawman's beautiful sister.

When one of Bat Masterson's friends is murdered, Bat is deputized as federal officer to conduct the suspect, outlaw Luke Steiger, to Fort Smith for trial. However, Steiger disappears on the night before he and Bat are to leave for their destination.

Sports-fan Bat Masterson fails to see eye to eye with the Denver Fire Department about the fire hazards of the Denver Athletic Club. Bat has just bought the institution - but the Fire Commissioner declares it a fire risk and orders it torn down.
Hardie tries to clear the name of a Mexican shepherd accused of abducting a young girl.
Jim goes undercover as a trainee for Wells Fargo to try and find out who is supplying renegade Indians with the guns they are using on their murderous raids.
Hardie isn't pleased when a dandy and beautiful woman insist on becoming passengers with him on the route he will use to deliver money to Laredo, as they fight off Mexican bandits along the way.
Sam Bass and his gang have a hideout in Texas so secluded that no lawman has ever located it. Hardie is ordered to infiltrate the gang so that he can learn where they hole up between raids.
In 1836 Davy Crockett, Jim Bowie and more than 180 Texans hold off the Mexican army for 13 days during Sam Houston's revolution.

The Sisters brothers find themselves on a journey through the Northwest, bringing them to the mountains of Oregon, a dangerous brothel in a small town and the gold rush land of California- an adventure that tests the deadly family ties that bind.
Marshal Cass Silver struggles to maintain peace as a trail herd approaches and enemies within the town plot to take him out, one way or another.
A Vega family friend is thrown in prison by the cruel new Comandante.
Galindo tries to trap Zorro by claiming that he's already caught him.
Bernardo overhears a plot to kill Diego's childhood sweetheart.
Another of The Eagle's agents stalks a gypsy tribe in search of their gold.
Outlaws pose as tax collectors to cover their crimes.
A determined preacher is robbed of the money he has raised to build a church.
Unsavory hoodlums oppose the filling in of a mosquito-laden bog.
Petitions calling for the removal of crooked officials are stolen.
After narrowly escaping a lynch mob, atheist gunslinger Ernie Parsons steals the identity of a murdered preacher he dicovers dead in the desert, and takes his job in a western town.
A rancher leads a posse to recover his kidnapped grandson from outlaws.

An injured cowboy on the run falls for the young daughter of a Quaker family that takes him in. As his violent past starts to catch up to him, he is forced to choose between his former life or a peaceful future.

program.episode_title: Man Who Shot Liberty Valance, The
program.synopsis: An experienced gunman and a peace-loving tenderfoot clash with a Western bully.

In the last days of the Wild West, Calamity Jane works as a muleskinner for Custer, has a fling with Wild Bill Hickok, and tries to reclaim her daughter when she travels to London with Buffalo Bill's Wild West Show.

In the last days of the Wild West, Calamity Jane works as a muleskinner for Custer, has a fling with Wild Bill Hickok, and tries to reclaim her daughter when she travels to London with Buffalo Bill's Wild West Show.
In Colorado territory, outlaw Wes McQueen escapes jail to pull a railroad robbery but, upon meeting pretty settler Julie Ann, he wonders about going straight.
A war veteran takes on an evil arthritic rancher and his murderous daughter to stop them from forcing out settlers and laying claim to their land.
Galindo tries to orchestrate the death of the new Comandante.

Two brothers join a cattle drive from Texas to Montana. While heading for Texas they save Nella from the Indians, and she decides to ride with them. Meanwhile, the cattle drivers must make one last trip through unwelcoming native territories.
A stagecoach stop employee and a stranded woman traveler find themselves at the mercy of four desperate outlaws intent on robbing the next day's gold shipment.
Two Army officers travel to Mexico on a secret mission to prevent a megalomaniacal ex-Confederate Colonel from selling a cache of stolen rifles to a band of murderous Apaches.
Young farmboy who always wanted to be a cowhand talks a tough trail boss into hiring him on a cattle drive.
Pacer Burton is the mixed-race son of a white rancher and his second wife, a Kiowa Indian. As struggle starts between the whites and the Kiowas, the Burton family is split between loyalties.
Marshal Cass Silver struggles to maintain peace as a trail herd approaches and enemies within the town plot to take him out, one way or another.
A Cavalry captain tries to keep the Indians from getting their hands on stolen Gatling Guns.
On the stagecoach to Cheyenne a mixed group of passengers must work together to survive the arduous journey and the Indian attacks.
A young man returns home to Arizona after several years' absence to find that a gang is after not only his family's ranch, but his girlfriend too.
Southern sympathizers attempt to sabotage the railroad construction efforts to build the Kansas Pacific Railway in the early 1860s just prior to the American Civil War.
After being robbed of a large sum of money, an embittered prospector denies his wife a purple silk dress which has become a symbol of beauty in her life.

Prostitutes Cody, Anita, Eileen and, work a saloon in the Old West. They decide to flee the bordello for a better life elsewhere, but trouble follows close behind. They're tailed by Pinkerton detectives, Cody's savings are stolen by a bandit, and Eileen is wrongfully jailed for bank robbery. Now the remaining three girls must decide whether to liberate their friend or their funds first.
An officer accused of cowardice volunteers to bring back General Custers's body after Little Big Horn.
New arrival Mario Arsatti becomes very friendly with Lucas, but this turns out to be merely pleasure before business: he's been hired to kill Lucas.
Lucas, who blames his wife's death on his father-in-law, refuses to help the old man elude gunmen.
A sheriff's son is about to rejoin an outlaw band after his release from prison.
The star witness against gang-boss Broderick is kidnapped.

With his fiancée arriving from London, Ron Browning will do anything to keep her from finding out what a failure he is... even die... at least on paper. But his courage in a later crisis may restore his self respect and save the Sheriff at the same time.

Paul Hayden is baited into a fight with three wild brothers, wounding one. When he later meets and falls in love with Karen Thomas, the sister of his three assailants, Hollister attempts to untangle the snarled affairs and prevent murder.

A young man adopted by a Spanish nobleman and is raised as his son and heir but he rebels and becomes a Franciscan monk. On his way to the San Gabriel Mission he gets lost and ends up in Death Valley.
Claim jumper Jennie attempts to jump the mining claim of Buck Hansen near Rhyolite, Nevada, and ends up rescuing Buck who is lost in Death Valley.
Although Adam Larkin has spent many years trying to make a living from his land, he has only met with failure. When the bank refuses to loan him money, he decides to take what he needs.
After discovering the murdered body of a rancher, Milo Brant tries to comfort Ruth, the grieving widow. Her son, however, distrusts Brant and resents the man's attention to his mother.

Longley and Captain Acosta of the Mexican Rurales travel to San Thomas to bid on a shipment of rifles and ammunition being sold at auction. There efforts are in vain - to their surprise, they are outbid by a beautiful blonde woman. They learn that she's merely the front for a notorious gun runner who plans to sell the guns along the border where they'll soon fall into the hands of the Apaches. Longley and Acosta throw into together to prevent the gunrunners plans from coming to fruition.

Stopping by a remote cabin to water his horse, Longley stumbles across its dead owner, the recipient of two bullets in the back. The dead man's brothers don't believe Longley's story that he just killed a rattlesnake and are preparing to string him up when he's rescued by a stranger with a dislike for lynchings. Longley explains his story to the marshal, who rides out of town to find the rattler, but Longley soon discovers that just about half the town was related to the dead man - and many of them are stone cold killers themselves more interested in revenge than explanations.
Champion of Justice Bat Masterson and lovely Lorna Adams race against time and the executioner in an unusual adventure triggered by a stagecoach hold up.

Beau-Brummel-of-the-Frontier, Bat Masterson, calls on his tailor at Junction, New Mexico. However, the well-known craftsman - newly chosen head of a Special Citizens Committee - is unable to serve Bat because of his political duties.
A schoolteacher comes to Gloribee and wins over the town – until his past is revealed.
Hijackers hold a wealthy young man and his fiancee for ransom.
While transporting Dan Lingle to stand trial for robbery and murder, Hardie's stagecoach is attacked by the outlaw's gang.
A lawman leads a posse in search of a bank robber, an old friend whose ex-wife he married.
The arrival of a hired gun stirs up suspicions in a small town.
In 1836 Davy Crockett, Jim Bowie and more than 180 Texans hold off the Mexican army for 13 days during Sam Houston's revolution.
A strong willed female saloon owner helps a wounded gang member, and is wrongly suspected of murder and bank robbery by a lynch mob.
A straight-shooting sheriff and a newspaperwoman take on a band of outlaws in the Oklahoma panhandle.
The notoriously lazy Jackman brothers discover their mortgage has been foreclosed.
Mark and his girlfriend Laurie discover that an old hermit is very lonely.
A bank robber posing as the Lone Ranger pays another man to confess.
A singer poses as a French opera star to attract a bigger gate.

In self defense, Sheriff Hollister gunned down the son of the richest rancher in the territory, who offers a reward for Clay's death. A Marshal of a neighboring town lures the sheriff out of town so he, himself, can kill Clay and claim the reward.

A wealthy rancher is killed and robbed of the ranch payroll. With only a horseshoe print to go by, Sheriff Hollister and Editor Claibourne set about trapping the culprit and end up meeting him in a furious fight.
Two men running from a posse find a rich silver deposit, now they must find a way to stake their claim without going to jail.
A group of Mormons decides to set up a settlement in Utah.
A drifter arrives in a small Western town claiming to be a famous lawman who lived there years before. The townspeople, who remember the lawman as a young and vigorous man, refuse to believe him.
Sheriff Parney hangs a local badman. Later the dead man's two sons vow to kill Parney in revenge.

Longley must help a parolee and his pretty daughter battle a family of outlaws who are trying to drive him off his ranch. Longley has a pair of unlikely allies - a young gunman dressed all in black and a half-crazed old woman who was long held as a prisoner by the Apaches.

After the surrender of the Army of Northern Virginia at Appomattox, Longley and two his junior officers agree to reunion five years later. The trio rendezvous in Laramie, Wyoming where the sheriff suspects one of the ex-Confederates of stealing an army-payroll.

Austrian nobleman Anton von Landi, short of funds on the American Frontier, holds up a gambling casino. Unfortunately for him, at the open end of his shooting iron is the redoubtable Bat Masterson, who's in the process of buying into the establishment.

Bat Masterson discovers that half of a great deal of money is still a great deal of money. With his usual skill, Bat is winning a poker game in Tucson when one of the players stakes half of a lottery ticket and Bat wins it. He's surprised to learn it's part of a winning ticket of high value.
Jeb, Hardie and Tina are caught in a mountain blizzard and must spend a terrifying night in a ghost town.

When a Wells Fargo train shipment is robbed in Missouri, Jesse James and his gang are suspected. Hardie disguises himself as a photographer to locate the money and the outlaws who killed his friend on the train.
Jim Hardie goes undercover in Rainbow’s End to investigate the murder of Wells Fargo agent Roy Fulton who was killed at the roulette table.
Desperately hoping to turn the tide of the war, a Confederate soldier steals $2,000,000 worth of Union gold bars and heads for the Mexican border, where he hopes to buy munitions and supplies.
A cowboy tries to support four orphan boys and a runaway girl, without his boss knowing it.
A woman sells her successful business in New Orleans to find the men who killed her father.
A saloonkeeper's partner frames him for gold robberies, with a woman caught in the middle.
A man arrives in Rawhide, Nevada and spends his last 10 dollars on a town lot. The lot winds up being a gold mine.
After saving a woman kidnapped by Indians, a cowboy discovers that there is a reward for her return, dead or alive, and has to fight off bounty hunters to get her back to civilization.
In Colorado territory, outlaw Wes McQueen escapes jail to pull a railroad robbery but, upon meeting pretty settler Julie Ann, he wonders about going straight.

Lucas McCain and his son, Mark, arrive in North Fork, New Mexico looking to start a new life. They come across a ranch for sale that is to their liking, and Lucas enters a Turkey Shoot in hopes of winning the down payment for the ranch.
Cattle baron Oat Jackford orders his two cowboys to run Lucas McCain and his son off their ranch.
An adopted boy mistakenly believes that his outlaw father is a hero.
As a prospector, the Lone Ranger seeks a criminal mastermind.

Sentenced to hang for a holdup murder, Reed Barker meets his fate at the gallows but when the rope breaks when the trap is sprung, he is freed under Territorial Law. Sheriff Hollister and Editor Claibourne must unravel the mystery.

Angered over several recent mine disasters, Sheriff Hollister wages a campaign for mine safety legislation with the aid of Editor Claibourne of the Tombstone Epitaph. In the process, they uncover a clever ruse to discredit the safety campaign.
Two slaves are given permission to go west and pan gold in order to earn their freedom.
The town drunk is trying to reform before his sister arrives.

Linc Hardaway has a father who is one of the wealthiest men in the West. In his efforts to prove that he can do anything as well as his father, Linc encounters one failure after another, until even the girl he loves loses respect for him. Then Linc's father is murdered and he is suspected of the crime.
Ellie Matson's husband, Jess, is a wanted outlaw. When she realizes that their son admires Jess, she decides to show him just what kind of man his father is.

Longley chases after Clay Thompson, a ruthless bounty hunter who is pursuit of a man falsely accused of bank robbery. Thompson won't believe the Texan when he explains that the wanted posters are being retracted and arranges with a crooked deputy sheriff to ensure that Bill won't interfere with his deadly vocation.

When an outlaw gang shoots up a town and robs the bank, Bill joins the posse to track the owlhoots down. When the outlaws cross into Mexico, the sheriff and his men return home, but Bill crosses the border to avenge the deaths of a pretty mother and daughter who had only just arrived in the West.

Most-Alive-Man-on-the-Frontier Bat Masterson is pleased to announce that reports of his death are much exaggerated. When he tries to with draw on his account in a Bonanza, Colorado bank, he learns the account was closed out and that he, himself is "deceased."
A crime wave sweeps Cheyenne, putting a burden upon the Territorial Administrator. He turns to the one man he knows can handle his problem - Bat Masterson.
Tina hopes that Chuck Evans can help her escape from her dull life as a teacher.
A gang lead by Belle Starr robs a train with Hardie on board. Hardie tries to lure the pretty outlaw with an offer she can't refuse.
Hardie has to fend off an Indian attack and retain his prisoner at the same time.

When notorious American gunslinger, The Montana Kid, staggers into the tiny Canadian hamlet of Barclay's Brush, life for the town's 17 inhabitants is about to get exciting. The Kid immediately gets into an unfortunate altercation with Jack, the town's surly blacksmith, which leads to the The Kid 'calling him out' for a showdown. But in a place totally ill-prepared to deal with a classic gun fight and without a single working pistol to be found, adhering to the code of the American Wild West may prove difficult.
A frontier widow aims to raise sheep despite a cattle rancher, in old Wyoming.

Two brothers join a cattle drive from Texas to Montana. While heading for Texas they save Nella from the Indians, and she decides to ride with them. Meanwhile, the cattle drivers must make one last trip through unwelcoming native territories.
A couple learn that politics and romance don't mix when they align themselves on opposite sides of a state-boundary dispute.
John Eldredge, the new director of the Carson City Mint, has trouble adjusting to life in the rugged Western town.
Two brothers on opposite sides of the law become embroiled in an Arizona range war between cattlemen and farmers.
Pacer Burton is the mixed-race son of a white rancher and his second wife, a Kiowa Indian. As struggle starts between the whites and the Kiowas, the Burton family is split between loyalties.
When Mark gets caught on a mountain ledge, a young outlaw risks his life to rescue him and breaks his leg doing so.
The notorious Shelton brothers ride into the town of North Fork to take revenge on an elderly Marshal who is now crippled and working for Lucas McCain.
It's a race against time to recover money stolen in a bank robbery.
A geologist is badly beaten when he uncovers land fraud.

Adam Kirby witnesses a murder in the livery stable; then makes the mistake of "shooting off his mouth" about it, leading to the killer coming after him. Sheriff Hollister "baits" a trap with Kirby and waits to trap the killer and save Kirby.

When a U.S. Marshal is ambushed while bringing in outlaw Kyle Dodge, he turns his captive over to Sheriff Hollister just before he dies, with the cryptic warning "Trust nobody." Forewarned, the Sheriff forestalls a clever attempt to free Dodge.
A young man won't stop hunting for gold - not even to woo a pretty girl.
The new schoolmarm's skill with a pistol makes her the target of a vicious rumor.
Jess Clark is an ex-lawman who has lost his sight. Determined to shoot accurately, Jess is learning to shoot at sounds.
A wanted criminal disrupts the serenity of ranch life for Kathy and Jed Lorimer. Fleeing from justice, the criminal decides to take Kathy with him.
An actor/cardsharp who cleaned out two men at poker is set up as the fall guy by a crooked express agent and his cohorts when Longley is ambushed and the payroll he is delivering stolen.

Though reluctant at first, Bill accepts the offer of his old friend Sheriff Ben Tildy to take the job as his deputy, even though several deputies have been killed over the past few years as two ruthless men have taken over the town, and the residents blame Tildy for the deputies' deaths.

In a community of reformed law breakers, Bat Masterson finds himself- reluctantly - their bank president. In his keeping are their savings and townsmen's right to continue to enjoy their new way of life.

Fifteen years after the fact, Bat Masterson finds himself involved in the Lincoln Assassination conspiracy. The niece of John Surratt, an acquitted suspect, begs Bat to prevent publication of scurrilous story concerning Surratt's involvement in the tragedy. This is Part One of a Two Part story.
Hardie joins a father searching for his son seeking a lost gold mine in Arizona's Superstition Mountains.
When Hardie witnesses a robbery, a young man hoping to get his partner off wants Hardie scared off or killed before the trial.
A Pony Express rider, Stan Blake, is found dead on a route. Hardie takes on Blake’s route in hopes of luring the murderers out in the open.
Jim is sent to negotiate a right-of-way for Wells Fargo stagecoaches through Sam Brundage's property as a shortcut.

After the Civil War, ranch owner Gil Hanley helps a stranger in desperate need of medical attention. Soon he is questioned by Union soldiers about the stranger, who turns out to be an ex-Confederate soldier. Hanley, also an ex-Confederate, is unjustly convicted as a conspirator and sentenced to life in a brutal desert prison in New Mexico.
A strong willed female saloon owner helps a wounded gang member, and is wrongly suspected of murder and bank robbery by a lynch mob.
A posse of ranchers and U.S. Cavalrymen storm Montezuma's Castle to rescue a girl abducted by renegade Indians.

program.episode_title: Legend Of The Lone Ranger, The
program.synopsis: The notorious Cavendish Gang ambushes an entire squad of Texas rangers, shooting every man in sight. But their aim wasn't true, and one ranger miraculously escapes death. With a little help from his Native American friend, Tonto, The Lone Ranger fights to ensure that justice prevails and the Old West remains safe for everyone.
The citizens of Gold Hill scorn a dancehall queen - until an epidemic strikes.
A new rifle in restricted use brings the threat of death to a pioneer family.
Southern sympathizers attempt to sabotage the railroad construction efforts to build the Kansas Pacific Railway in the early 1860s just prior to the American Civil War.
To make amends for accidentally killing a young boy, a Civil War veteran embarks on a dangerous journey into Apache territory to lay the boy in his final resting place.
Johnny Gibbs, Lucas McCain's brother-in-law, visits the McCain ranch. Gibbs, a rodeo rider wanted by the law, begins stirring up trouble.
A vengeful judge who believes Lucas was responsible for his son's death, kidnaps Mark McCain to revenge himself on Lucas.
An Easterner thinks the Durango Kid is her long-lost brother.
A young army deserter regrets his actions and joins the Lone Ranger to redeem himself.

In order to trap an outlaw gang, the Epitaph reports that a gang member is turning state's witness. The outlaw dies of his wounds, but his death is kept secret. In a bold move, the gang kidnaps Claibourne and offers to exchange him for the outlaw.

The Editor witnesses a murder, identifying Vic Reel as the killer. Hollister arrests Reel, but before the trial, gang leader Jason Douglas sends triggers to kill Claibourne, setting the stage for a big shoot out in the streets of Tombstone.
A woman in love with a fire chief has a rival: a flashy fire engine.
The story of Lotta Crabtree, the little girl who became "The Golden West's Gift to Vaudeville."
Dr. Mike Reynolds is faced with a major decision: to treat critically ill gunslinger, Dolf Akins, who once terrorized the town, or give in to the townspeople's wishes and let him die.
David Manning , returning from the Civil War, seeks out Adam Stewart. Manning wishes to obtain a deposition from Stewart stating that he served in Stewart's place during the war.
Bill stops a trail boss from harassing a saloon girl. The humiliated trail boss then decides to use a young man, too eager to prove his manhood and join up with the group, to get his revenge on Bill.

Bill tries to help a young woman who is being pursued by two men. The men tell him that she is a thief and that they are bringing her back to the gunfighter who she is trying to flee from. Unfortunately her experience with the gunfighter has made her bitter toward all men.

In Part II of the Lincoln Assassination story, Bat Masterson discovers that an imposture has been committed in order to plant a scandalous story in a newspaper. He reveals bartender John Watson as the scoundrel - and, almost immediately after, finds Watson dead - a fact pointing to his own mortal danger.
Priceless black pearls, a masterly train robbery and a murderous cellmate figure in Bat Masterson's adventure resulting from being jailed on suspicion of cheating at cards.
Jim Hardie investigates a stagecoach holdup where white men posing as Indians kill the driver and guard and left a pretty passenger for dead.
Hardie is suspicious when a man and a woman bid vigorously on a battered suitcase.

Jim Hardie is called in when a stagecoach is robbed near the Arizona/New Mexico border. He is told that the only thing taken was a yellow mongrel dog. Investigating further he learns that the dog's collar may provide evidence to a murder.
Jim Hardie and Wells Fargo agents set a trap for the notorious Stillwell gang, but the outlaws prove to be more than a match for the detectives.

Infamous outlaw, Ben Wade, is captured by the law and rancher Dan Evans finds himself part of the posse sworn in to escort Wade to the 3:10 to Yuma train that will transport him to prison and a certain death sentence.
A retired Texas Ranger and his three aging pals help to clean up a town run by a crooked mayor, a drunken judge and a trigger-happy sheriff.

program.episode_title: Over The Hill Gang Rides Again, The
program.synopsis: Four retired Texas Rangers leap to the aid of their old chum the Baltimore Kid who has become a hopeless drunk, and they aim to clean him up and install him as Marshal of Waco, Texas. But in order to complete the job, the geriatric quartet will have to help the Kid defeat the outlaw gang that wants him dead.
A bounty hunter escorts a killer to town to be hanged, but is allowing the man's outlaw brother to catch up with him for a showdown over a shocking previous murder.
Galindo tries to steal gunpowder from the cuartel.
Galindo makes a last-ditch attempt to silence Toledano once and for all.
The Eagle takes steps to assure his conquest of Southern California.
It's Tornado to the rescue when Diego is used as bait to capture Zorro.
Crooks steal irrigation project funds, then frame a dead man.
Dan Reid saves a gang leader's life.
The Lone Ranger finds a boy who ran away from home with his pony.
A girl who witnessed a murder helps the Lone Ranger capture a gang of robbers.

When notorious American gunslinger, The Montana Kid, staggers into the tiny Canadian hamlet of Barclay's Brush, life for the town's 17 inhabitants is about to get exciting. The Kid immediately gets into an unfortunate altercation with Jack, the town's surly blacksmith, which leads to the The Kid 'calling him out' for a showdown. But in a place totally ill-prepared to deal with a classic gun fight and without a single working pistol to be found, adhering to the code of the American Wild West may prove difficult.
Young farmboy who always wanted to be a cowhand talks a tough trail boss into hiring him on a cattle drive.

Recently rescued from an Indian ambush, a Southern loner rides into town and wins the deed to the local saloon in a card game against its menacing card-cheat owner. With secession causing tensions to rise between Denver's Northerner majority and Southerner minority, he is pressured to support his Confederate brothers, but he remains firmly committed to his one true cause - gold!
The arrival of a hired gun stirs up suspicions in a small town.
A lawman leads a posse in search of a bank robber, an old friend whose ex-wife he married.
A woman learns to shoot in order to exact vengeance on the men who raped her and killed her husband.
A Pinkerton detective goes undercover to infiltrate a gang of thieves whose boss is a feisty lady saloonkeeper.
A frontier widow aims to raise sheep despite a cattle rancher, in old Wyoming.
A young man returns home to Arizona after several years' absence to find that a gang is after not only his family's ranch, but his girlfriend too.
Raquel enacts her own plot to steal the cuartel's gunpowder.
In 1836 Davy Crockett, Jim Bowie and more than 180 Texans hold off the Mexican army for 13 days during Sam Houston's revolution.
Betrayed by his best friend, a cowboy must protect his herd from rustlers and risk losing his fiancee.

Pioneer life in the 1870s New Mexico territory was never easy for Jane Hammond and her ex-outlaw husband "Ham." But when Ham is shot by the ruthless John Bishop and his gang, who are ready to lay siege to the Hammond homestead, Jane's last hope lies with a world-weary gunslinger-and former lover.

Prostitutes Cody, Anita, Eileen and, work a saloon in the Old West. They decide to flee the bordello for a better life elsewhere, but trouble follows close behind. They're tailed by Pinkerton detectives, Cody's savings are stolen by a bandit, and Eileen is wrongfully jailed for bank robbery. Now the remaining three girls must decide whether to liberate their friend or their funds first.

Infamous outlaw, Ben Wade, is captured by the law and rancher Dan Evans finds himself part of the posse sworn in to escort Wade to the 3:10 to Yuma train that will transport him to prison and a certain death sentence.

The Sisters brothers find themselves on a journey through the Northwest, bringing them to the mountains of Oregon, a dangerous brothel in a small town and the gold rush land of California- an adventure that tests the deadly family ties that bind.
Two very different women become a bank-robbing duo in an effort to combat a ruthless enforcer terrorizing their Mexican town.

When notorious American gunslinger, The Montana Kid, staggers into the tiny Canadian hamlet of Barclay's Brush, life for the town's 17 inhabitants is about to get exciting. The Kid immediately gets into an unfortunate altercation with Jack, the town's surly blacksmith, which leads to the The Kid 'calling him out' for a showdown. But in a place totally ill-prepared to deal with a classic gun fight and without a single working pistol to be found, adhering to the code of the American Wild West may prove difficult.

Two brothers join a cattle drive from Texas to Montana. While heading for Texas they save Nella from the Indians, and she decides to ride with them. Meanwhile, the cattle drivers must make one last trip through unwelcoming native territories.
After arriving in Texas to escape a scandal back east, a lawyer becomes involved in the independence of Texas.

A handsome Italian count arrives in North Fork. The town bully ridicules his manner of speech and dress, and provokes the Count to challenge him to a duel. The Count refuses to back out and arranges a surprise for the town bully.

A new bank opens in North Fork, and Banker Hamilton hires a brawny, slow-witted gunman to guard the safe. Complications arise when some old outlaw friends of the gunman arrive in town and propose that he join them in robbing the bank.
The Lone Ranger must solve a murder committed by a team of gamblers.
An aide plots to kill a territorial governor.

Rancher Bert Magraw returns from Denver with his young bride Jeannie. The same day, notorious gunslinger Vince Sanders arrives in town and baits Magraw into a gunfight. Sheriff Hollister steps in to save Magraw's life and uncover a conspiracy.

Convicted of slugging and robbing a miner, Renee Carter is being taken to Yuma prison. When the miner dies, she must return for trial, but Hollister believes she is covering up for a gambler, Frank Fallon, who will try to prevent her return.

John "Snowshoe" Thompson, the first man to carry the mail via skis over the high mountain passes during the California gold rush, is engaged but tells his fiancee he's got to carry the mail one more winter and then they'll wed.
A gold town celebrates the discovery of a nugget that weighs 12 pounds.
Grant Coburn learns that his son has been hired as a professional gunslinger. He is faced with a choice between saving an innocent man's life or shooting down his own son.
Blacksmith Jonas Sutton is elected sheriff. After he takes office, the townsfolk pressure him into hunting down a murderer who previously saved Sutton's son.

Bill stops at an inn, where he meets an old lawman friend, who is holding two convicted killers. The lawman knows that friends of his two prisoners are in the area and planning to free them. When the lawman is wounded, Bill must take the responsibility of keeping the prisoners in custody himself, helped only by the meek owner of the inn, his domineering wife, and a gambler unwilling to take unnecessary risks.

While Bill is participating in a draw and shoot contest in a town rodeo, the local bank is robbed, and a photographer friend of Bill's is shot and killed. The photographer's young son, however, sees the picture his father took of the robbers just before they shot him.

Spanish gold and fabulous jewels lure many parties of treasure hunters to distraction in the Colorado Territory. Bat Masterson turns Anders Dorn and niece Elsa from their intended hazardous expedition in search of the legendary Santa Lucia, a fabulous Spanish plate ship rumored lost in the Colorado River.

Never is Bat Masterson's chivalry so sorely tested as when he's offered a number of valuable gifts - including a beautiful girl. This is in reward for services to an old gypsy whom he rescued from a hot-tempered Dodge City rancher.
Maxon and Hardie try to prevent a young boy from becoming an outlaw.
Michael Landon stars as a Fargo messenger accused of murder.
Jim Hardie must prove that a Wells Fargo ship captain is innocent when $20,000 worth of jade is stolen in the Barbary Coast.
A stranger's questions about a hanged Indian make him unwelcome in a California oil town.
After a gold prospector is killed by masked robbers, a detective is hired to find the surviving killer as well as the prospector's legal inheritors.
A U.S. tycoon hires an 1880s gunfighter to find his brother in Mexico.
A former sheriff returns home from the Civil War to find everyone thought he was dead, and his sweetheart engaged to his land-grabbing buddy.
Age is no object to a lovelorn man who buys a toupee and a new set of teeth to win a dance-hall girl.
Two very different women become a bank-robbing duo in an effort to combat a ruthless enforcer terrorizing their Mexican town.

Prostitutes Cody, Anita, Eileen and, work a saloon in the Old West. They decide to flee the bordello for a better life elsewhere, but trouble follows close behind. They're tailed by Pinkerton detectives, Cody's savings are stolen by a bandit, and Eileen is wrongfully jailed for bank robbery. Now the remaining three girls must decide whether to liberate their friend or their funds first.

Mark gets his father Lucas in trouble, when he tries to play Cupid between Lucas and a pretty young stranger who arrives in North Fork. The girl's two older brothers accuse him of trifling with her affections and challenge him to fight them both.

A New Orleans arch-criminal named Tiffauges, having been run out of Louisiana, is passing through the North Fork area with his entourage. Spotting the McCain ranch, he decides it would make a ranch for himself and tells McCain he wants to buy it.
A gambler exposes robbers who framed his son.
The Lone Ranger saves home owners who have built their house on the spot where thieves have buried their loot.

Returning to Tombstone with lawyer Gay Monahan, Hollister is just in time to prevent a gunfight between mine owner Big Jim Gerson and his manager, Feeny Spindler. Spindler hires the lady lawyer who digs up some surprising facts and a frozen body.

Hollister investigates a series of mysterious deaths among the silver miners and links Lily Murdock, the town's most respected woman, with the tragedies. Lily is so well liked by the townspeople that the sheriff dares not accuse her openly.
A young miner is afflicted with a monumental problem - claustrophobia.
A mining engineer tries to negotiate with Indians who won't allow a railroad to cross their lands.
Regan Moore, discontented with her marriage, attracts the love of gunman Dix Porter. Feeling sure of Porter's love, she tries to persuade him to kill her husband, Jud.
Following the Civil War, Ben Sanderson returns to his home in the South only to be greeted by angry former friends.

Riding with a posse, Bill captures the bank robber they were after, as well as the money he had stolen. But the others in the posse decide to split the loot among themselves. When Bill is the only one who refuses to go along with the idea, they decide they'll have to kill him as well as the robber. But a band of Comancheros interrupts their plans.

Bill is ambushed and his horse is stolen. Having to take a stagecoach, he finds that one of the other passengers is wanted outlaw Pony Sloan. But after stopping at a way station, he meets another man from whom he finds there is another side to Sloan's story.

Daredevil Bat Masterson risks his scalp on an expedition to buy buffalo hides in dangerous Indian country. Luke Simes, the man originally assigned to the job, had made some unflattering remarks about Bat's method of dress. As a result, Simes suddenly finds himself "unable to travel."

Bat is convinced that a painting purchased by a beautiful friend is a forgery since the original burned. He tries to prove the painting is fake but finds himself on the wrong side of a bet and framed for the murder of a renown art critic.
Hardie and his prisoner fight off gunmen who want to kill them both.

General Sheridan, who hates Indians, is escorted by Jim Hardie by stagecoach to Fort Laramie for a meeting with the Sioux tribe to create peace in the area. Attacking outlaws may ensure that the powwow never comes to pass.
Rather than spend Christmas with friends as he planned, Jim Hardie is sent to track gunrunners who are transporting weapons illegally across the Mexican border at Laredo.
Betrayed by his best friend, a cowboy must protect his herd from rustlers and risk losing his fiancee.

Pioneer life in the 1870s New Mexico territory was never easy for Jane Hammond and her ex-outlaw husband "Ham." But when Ham is shot by the ruthless John Bishop and his gang, who are ready to lay siege to the Hammond homestead, Jane's last hope lies with a world-weary gunslinger-and former lover.
After their service in the Civil War, four brothers go their separate ways, but later find themselves on opposite sides of a final showdown.
Fess Parker plays a marshal patrolling the New Mexico land rush of 1895.
A blind girl is instrumental in the search for robbers.
After serving a prison sentence, a farmer returns to his Pacific Northwest gold rush town to retrieve his adolescent son who was left in the care of a dance hall singer.
An unemployed cowhand is hired by his dishonest friend as muscle in a dispute between homesteaders and a cattleman.

Marshal Torrance has to go out of town on business and appoints the young son of the local storekeeper as his deputy in his absence. Lucas has to step in to protect the young deputy, when cowhands come gunning for revenge.

Lucas and Mark are riding the stage, returning from a big cattle sale, when the stage is robbed by three outlaws. Lucas, desperate to recover the money which is his entire year's profits, determines to pursue the outlaws.
Two thieves posing as representatives of the railroad try to get an old man to sell his land to them.
The Lone Ranger helps a stage line manager to deal with a rival who wants to bankrupt the line.

Marshal Vern Fawcett turns in his badge after accidentally shooting an outlaw in the back. When Holcomb's three vengeful brothers ride into Tombstone, Sheriff Hollister helps Fawcett regain his courage for the fight against lawlessness.

Hollister rushes a young, wounded hoodlum to Tombstone's hospital, putting him under the care of nurse Mary Rice. But when the notorious Gayleyville gang kidnaps her and the dying boy, Hollister and the wounded man each attempt to save the nurse.
