An irresponsible husband forces his family to get along without him, and they do - all too well.
To win the hand of a tycoon's daughter, a reporter must amass a fortune in three days.
Ranching a cattle drive, cowhands find a young boy wandering along the trail.
When Sam Carter refuses to accept his share of stolen goods, he inadvertently places his wife's life in jeopardy.

After killing one of the outlaw Kiley brothers, the citizens of Calico have buried him as an exhibit in the middle of the main street. The veteran lawman who they've hired as their new sheriff objects to this provocative act, causing the citizens to ask for his resignation. Then Bill comes to warn the citizens after he learns that the Kiley gang is planning to ride in and wipe out the entire town in revenge.
Bill is working with a telegraph company installing lines, but an outlaw gang camped in the area wants to get the crew to leave.

Bat seeks to collect a loan given to a reformed gunman who is now a rancher. In order to get his money he must save the man from a trigger-happy sheriff who believes the man is guilty of murder due to a witness's story.

An old friend asks Bat to San Francisco to look into the background of her daughter's fiancé. Bat learns she is a frequent visitor at a casino where she seems to win but her fiancé seems to lose - even his life.
Stagecoach passengers, including a notorious gunfighter, are menaced by outlaws.
When a Wells Fargo office is robbed and one of Jim Hardie's friends is murdered, Hardie is intent on exposing the criminals responsible.

Pretending to be a hardened convict, Hardie escapes from prison with a felon who stashed the proceeds from a stagecoach robbery before he was captured, in hopes that his new partner will lead him to the hidden loot.
A cattle baron fights to tame both the West and his estranged wife who deserted him and returns ready to battle over their daughter's future.

program.episode_title: Good Guys And The Bad Guys, The
program.synopsis: An aging lawman and an aging outlaw join forces when their respective positions in society are usurped by a younger but incompetent Marshal, and a younger but vicious gang leader.

Larry, Moe, and Curly Joe work for an editor at a Boston wildlife conservation magazine and make such a mess of the pressroom that their publisher gets rid of them by sending them out west to stop the slaughter of buffalo. Upon their arrival they find themselves being sought after by every notorious gunslinger in history. Luckily, pistol-packing Annie Oakley, who has fallen in love with the handsome editor, agrees to protect them against the bad guys.
There's trouble among the Indians when a member of one tribe steals a venerated portrait from neighboring braves.
A politician tries to get Columbia picked as California's capital city.

Mountain man and fur trapper Flint Mitchell is planning a hunting trek into the beaver-rich Blackhawk Territory, despite protests from his Indian friend who tells him that the land belongs to Indians.

A penniless miner comes to the aid of a man being attacked by two men he claims were trying to steal his land. However, he soon discovers things are not quite so clear cut, and that the apparent victim of the crime may have actually been the perpetrator.
Lucas gets in trouble when he accuses the foreman of a neighboring ranch of rustling some of his cattle.

An Argentine family moves to North Fork and settles on a ranch adjoining Lucas'. The Argentinean adult immigrant is openly hostile in return, particularly toward a young North Fork boy who pays court to the settlers' daughter.
The Lone Ranger nearly walks into a blasting area set up by crooks to protect their hidden gold.
The Lone Ranger takes the place of a young boy seeking revenge.

Fragment of the Epitaph's are found at the scene of a robbery and on the body of a known killer. Hollister and Claibourne discover a strange code in the help wanted ads and with a planned typographic error, they smoke out a robbery gang.

Young and beautiful Liz Dolthan stands by as Hollister opens a safe to deposit personal valuables belonging to the stage coach passengers. When the safe is robbed, Hollister devises a ruse to make Liz reveal the fact that she has an amazing memory.
The romance of young lovers is threatened by their feuding families.
A teacher stages a holdup as a practical joke, but it works so well that he decides to make a career of it.
Gino Pelleti, an Italian immigrant tailor, is saddened by the discovery that he is losing his son's respect.

Frank Gilette, a notorious gunman just out of prison, is framed for a bank robbery. Convicted of the charge, Gilette is on his way to prison, accompanied by the sheriff and his deputy, when gunmen kill the sheriff and take Gilette and the deputy to a hideout.

Bill is hired as trail boss driving a cattle herd to Abilene. But he has to contend with two big problems. One is a hostile trail hand, whose anger over Bill getting the ramrod job over him is aggravated by his jealousy over the ranch owner's daughter. The other is a rival drive who's leader will stop at nothing to insure that his herd gets to Abilene first. First of a two part story, concluding with "Showdown At Abilene".

After failing in an attempt to ambush Bill, Bishop pays a band of Indians to attack the Akins herd, hoping to scatter the cattle and thus prevent them from reaching Abilene first. Conclusion of a two-part story, the first part being "Stampede".
Bat races to the bedside of a dying friend, only to discover the man is quite well and needs Bat's help in his quest to relocate the Stone County seat from Bundy to Sapphira.

While visiting St. Joseph, Missouri to see his former girlfriend, an up-and-coming soprano, Bat comes to the aid of a businessman who refuses to pay protection money but finds himself accused of being an enforcer for the racket.

Jim Hardie agrees to having Wells Fargo take a woman as cargo who is wanted as a material witness in a San Francisco political corruption investigation after the detective assigned to escort her is murdered.

A young man stranded in a remote town is looking for a job working with horses. Jim Hardie befriends him along with a cranky softy and he returns the favor by helping Hardie solve the murder of a Wells Fargo agent.

Jim Hardie attempts to find a gang of outlaws who specialize in stealing buffalo hides from Fresno Keeley, a buyer backed by Wells Fargo. He suspects one of those involved is Billy Thompson, brother of Ben Thompson.
Jim Hardie joins forces with wanted outlaw Bill Longley to retrieve money stolen from Wells Fargo, however another outlaw working with Longley tricks Hardie and steals the money and Longley’s girl.
A man enters a town and poses as a hangman, but he has intentions of freeing a gang of outlaws, including his brother, from the gallows.

Indian scout Tom Jeffords is sent out to stem the war between the Whites and Apaches in the late 1870s. He learns that the Indians kill only to protect themselves, or out of retaliation for white atrocities. Befriending the sagacious Apache leader Cochise, Jeffords ensures safe passage for white mail-carriers through Indian territory. As he becomes closer to the Native Americans, Jeffords falls in love with and weds a pretty Apache girl.
A bounty hunter trying to bring a murderer to justice is forced to accept the help of two less-than-trustworthy strangers.
A judge attempts to bring a killer to justice in a small wild west town. Unfortunately the town is owned by the suspected killer's father.
Two men fight to rid Virginia City of its corrupt judicial system.

program.episode_title: To Big Charlie From Little Charlie
program.synopsis: Two Charlies - one miner is on his way up, the other is on his way down.
A gunfighter turns rancher at the mercy of a son who hates him and a gambler who wants to kill him.
Stage coach passengers and outlaws travelling through Indian country are forced to join forces against the Apaches.
Lucas and Mark are exposed to a deadly disease when they adopt a stray horse.

After the end of the Civil War, General Phil Sheridan, who has been appointed Governor of the New Mexico Territory, is on an inspection tour of the country. Trouble occurs when the General is confronted by an ex-confederate soldier.
Rivals are after a couple's stagecoach franchise.
A schoolteacher uses his profession as a cover for his real job as a bank robber.
Sheriff Hollister faces off against a brother and his beautiful sister, both of whom are looking to avenge the death of their notorious outlaw father, Jonas Bell and Hollister is the target.

It's Sheriff Clay Hollister's duty to hang murderer Sam Edwards. However, on the fateful morning his brother appears armed with a case of dynamite. Saving Sam from the gallows, his brother abducts Editor Harris Claibourne to hold him hostage.
Sequoia, a Cherokee, is the object of ridicule as he painstakingly tries to develop an alphabet for his people.
The story of Lola Montez, the legendary dancer of the 1800s.
A former confederate soldier journeys to the transcontinental railroad to seek vengeance.
Cullen fights for his life as he answers for his actions; Lily struggles to survive in the wilderness; Durant attempts to spin tragedy for political gain.
Cullen is diverted in the search of the sergeant; Durant organizes a search party to find Lily Bell.
Cullen closes in on the sergeant; Durant has a hidden agenda while helping Lily recuperate.
Durant's answer to the railroad's financial problems spurs a brawl between Cullen and Elam.
After a quiet drifter is met with violence and bullying in a small town, he finds himself in a vengeful struggle with the local marshal.

On the run in the American Southwest, a boy encounters the infamous outlaw Billy the Kid and the legendary lawman Pat Garrett. He soon finds himself caught in the crossfire as Billy and Garrett square off in a deadly game of cat and mouse.
A cynical former sheriff turned bounty hunter helps a young, recently appointed acting sheriff with his advice, his experience and his gun.
Raquel questions her commitment to The Eagle's plan.
Diego intervenes when a boy challenges a dangerous stranger to a duel.
Diego and Bernardo investigate Murrieta's suspicious intentions.
Murrieta's brother arrives in Los Angeles with a deadly new weapon.
The Lone Ranger refuses to be fooled by crooks' plans to keep intruders away from their hideout.
Gamblers murder a man who hasn't paid his debts and pin the crime on an innocent derelict.
A man is framed a second time for stage robbery.
The inside of a ventriloquist's dummy is the hiding place for a stolen diamond.
A Chicago hotel clerk yearns for the Old West until he discovers, first hand, that the cowboy life is not what he'd imagined.
Zorro defends a young boy caught up in the Murrieta's smuggling scheme.
Diego must defend his own home when the new administrado arrives.

Monte Walsh is a cowboy in 1890s Wyoming, who finds calls for his expertise waning, as civilisation advances west. Should he head out for the last remaining wide open spaces, or stay with his sweetheart and take a job with the travelling Wild West show run by Colonel Wilson?
The Sackett brothers seek their fortunes out West after the Civil War.
The Sackett brothers seek their fortunes out West after the Civil War.

Wanderer Rafe Covington swears an oath to his dying best friend to look after his beloved wife and Wyoming ranch, only to encounter other forces who strongly desire the ranch and the woman for their own purposes.
A Confederate soldier returns to his Arizona homestead and finds Union sympathizers have laid claim to his land.

Brothers who battled on opposing sides of the Civil War return home to discover that their family has been kidnapped by a marauding band of rebel guerrillas who refuse to accept the defeat of the Confederacy.

A penniless miner comes to the aid of a man being attacked by two men he claims were trying to steal his land. However, he soon discovers things are not quite so clear cut, and that the apparent victim of the crime may have actually been the perpetrator.
A cynical former sheriff turned bounty hunter helps a young, recently appointed acting sheriff with his advice, his experience and his gun.
A gunslinger helps an ungrateful town fight off a raid by his former gang.
A Union deserter helps a group of women fight off a Comanche attack.
Notorious gunfighter Jimmy Ringo rides into town to find his true love, who doesn't want to see him. He hasn't come looking for trouble, but trouble finds him around every corner.
A vicious outlaw kidnaps a sheriff and forces him to lead a motley group of adventurers to a cache of Apache gold.
When an Army scout retires to a farm in New Mexico, he takes pity on a white woman and her half-breed son recently rescued from Indians, and invites them to join him.
A former bank robber searches for his double-crossing partner who left him for dead, while having to look after a 6-year-old girl.
A man is chasing four outlaws who killed his wife and finds them in a small town's jail, but they escape to Mexico.
Beautiful, biracial Pearl Chavez becomes the ward of her dead father's first love and finds herself torn between two brothers, one good and the other bad.

On the run in the American Southwest, a boy encounters the infamous outlaw Billy the Kid and the legendary lawman Pat Garrett. He soon finds himself caught in the crossfire as Billy and Garrett square off in a deadly game of cat and mouse.
After a quiet drifter is met with violence and bullying in a small town, he finds himself in a vengeful struggle with the local marshal.

A famous gunfighter decides to retire when he marries. His wife persuades him to move to North Fork, open a store, and settle down. The couple is involved in trouble when three outlaws arrive in town. When he refuses to respond, they wreck his store.

An old friend of Lucas, a traveling photographer, comes to North Fork plying his trade. When a murder is committed. He is about to be convicted on circumstantial evidence, when Mark and his father use photographs to unmask the real killer.
Thieves steal marked money.
Tonto and the Lone Ranger are asked to help an innocent man who's accused of murder.

Three holdup men clean out the Tombstone bank and as he goes to question a local farmer, Hollister is kidnapped. Epitaph editor Harris Claibourne and Hollister's deputy come to terms with the holdup men as a result of a startling revelation.

Outlaw Frank Banta and his outlaws kidnap the Territorial Governor and hold him hostage for the release of a condemned man. When Sheriff Hollister surrenders himself in exchange for the Governor, Banta prepares to hang them both.
The Borax 20-Mule Teams are center stage in this tale of a young Eastern tenderfoot who signs up to be "swamper" with the Big Team to prove his manliness to the girl he loves.
Tax duties provide a deputy with some strange experiences.

During a bank raid, Miguel and his gang get away with several thousand pesos in gold. But Miguel is betrayed by one of his men, who steals the gold, takes Miguel's woman and her younger brother, and escapes to a hiding place in the mountains.

Cason Thomas, a derelict, is held in contempt by most of his fellow townspeople. When the new sheriff, Johnny Ringo, befriends him and offers to make him a deputy, the old man feels he has an opportunity to gain new respect.

Still leading the cattle drive, Bill is allowed to take his herd across a powerful rancher's land. On the way across, he finds a woman who claims she is fleeing from the rancher's advances. But he soon learns that she has not told the whole truth.

A former lawman, now a storekeeper, has lost his nerve after a shooting incident, and is now being harassed by a gang of outlaws led by the brother of a man he once killed. The ex-lawman's daughter refuses to let him put on a gun again or try to fight back.

When an honest man is elected to be the town sheriff campaigning for fair taxes, the wealthy railroad owner ships his cash out of the county to avoid paying his fair share. Bat provides the new sheriff with help to collect the taxes.

When Bat learns that the two thousand dollars the Bassett brothers owe him is in jeopardy because of the shooting feud between the Bassetts and the Clements, Bat decides to intervene to protect his investment.
A gunman is bailed out of jail by a robber seeking an ally.

Hardie offers an old outlaw he sent to prison the opportunity to earn parole and a reward if he will lead a posse into an outlaw stronghold and help rescue a United States senator from his kidnappers.

Hardie desperately attempts to rescue the pretty woman doctor an outlaw gang abducted to help one of their wounded confederates, as the local Wells Fargo agent's wife needs her assistance during a difficult childbirth.
A U.S. marshal and his helper cross Texas by wagon with four prisoners, one a dance-hall hostess.
Raised since boyhood by a Pawnee chief, a young white man known as Pale Arrow finds his loyalty tested when he returns to his people and takes a job as a wagon train scout.
Chicago detectives in the 1880s head West to find the killers of their colleague.
A half-breed gunslinger and a friend he hasn't seen in years join together to escort a shipment of explosives across Utah.
A prospector in San Francisco to deposit his gold encounters the temptations of the big city.
The Sackett brothers seek their fortunes out West after the Civil War.
The Sackett brothers seek their fortunes out West after the Civil War.

A young couple that is getting married cause trouble for the whole town of North Fork, while traveling on a wagon train. Eloping, they plan to wed at her parents' home in Utah. Townsfolk decide to give the couple an old fashioned shivaree celebration, but the drunken crowd gets out of hand roughing up the bride, prompting the young groom to challenge them with a rifle. Lucas, sympathetic towards their plight, must prevent bloodshed.

McCain befriends Donnel O'Mahoney, a tough kid from Brooklyn, and arranges with two men for the young man to continue his travels on their wagon, but then one of them is murdered and the other accuses the boy of having done it.
Former Marshal Frank Dean thinks he lost his courage.
The U.S. Army sets out to keep guns away from hostile Indian tribes.

When Belle Reed comes to Tombstone from Philadelphia looking for excitement and finds it by falling in love with wanted murderer Ben Quaid, her twin sister arrives from the East to rescue her. But all may be fair in love and love.

Sheriff Clay Hollister encounters three escaped criminals who have just discovered a fortune in silver. They capture him and plan his death to protect their secret. With no guns and his hands tied, Hollister must think fast in order to survive.
As a cruel joke, two prospectors offer a friend shares in their mine if he'll marry the homely Aggie Filene.
A prospector strikes it rich and decides to marry the girl he left behind - unaware that she's stopped waiting.
A murder is committed and the chief suspect flees town. Sam Walston leads a band of vigilantes in pursuit of the man.

After many years away from his family, Dave Blasingame is asked to return home. Dave makes the trip, unaware that his only relative has been killed and his family's land has been seized by a ruthless tyrant.
Bill travels to Ciudad Juarez to accept a job working for a cattleman and his fiancée, only to soon find out he has been set up as the scapegoat in a counterfeiting scheme.

A rancher warns Bill and the railroad workers not to put track down through the land his cattle graze on, even though it is government land. Later he shoots and seriously wounds one of the workers as they are riding in a wagon. But Bill picks up the rancher's son instead, and the workers are in lynching mood.

Bat's stake is stolen to force him to work for a crooked casino owner. When he refuses, events lead Bat to come to the aid of two beautiful women who are being blackmailed by the owner of Sacramento's largest casino, "The Lady Luck."

When Bat buys a derby hat in a small Missouri town he is aghast at the price. Learning that a freight line owner forces shopkeepers to use only his line to ship their supplies from the river, Bat decides to break his monopoly.
A mule driver's wife and child are kidnapped by outlaws.

Jim Hardie delivers a race horse to a Wells Fargo employee to use in the Oklahoma Strip to stake out a Wells Fargo depot. When there is no word from him, Hardie is sent to locate the missing man and the horse.

After a train robbery, Jim Hardie is sent to guard a $20,000 payroll. An outlaw gang abducts him and one of the outlaws, using Jim's identification papers, takes his place to rob the local Wells Fargo safe with the help of the female clerk.
A cavalry major makes peace with Indians despite an agitator working for carpetbaggers.
Gold thieves and the government rile Geronimo, despite a cavalry captain's efforts at peace.

Mountain man and fur trapper Flint Mitchell is planning a hunting trek into the beaver-rich Blackhawk Territory, despite protests from his Indian friend who tells him that the land belongs to Indians.

Sheriff Chadbourne transports convict Ben Trask to El Paso in a covered wagon. The wagon also carries sea captain Theodore Bess and married couple Laura and Jerry Niblett. The group comes upon a wounded Native American, banished by his tribe, who offers to lead them to gold. But then the tribe attacks the wagon. Ben is freed to help, but the group's troubles have only begun.
Wagontrain members oppose their leader's desire to observe Sunday as a day of rest.
The miners become hostile when they suspect an assayer of cheating them.
Chicago detectives in the 1880s head West to find the killers of their colleague.

Brothers who battled on opposing sides of the Civil War return home to discover that their family has been kidnapped by a marauding band of rebel guerrillas who refuse to accept the defeat of the Confederacy.

U.S. Marshal Sam Buckhart arrives in North Fork in search of renegade Indians suspected of the brutal killing of a Texas Ranger and his family, but the townspeople are suspicious of him when they discover that he too is an Indian.

Reformed card sharp Julia Massini now runs a respectable boarding house in North Fork, but Sid Fallon is threatening to reveal her sordid past unless she lets him turn the establishment into a saloon and gambling hall.
A bandit terrorizes a woman who witnessed a robbery.
The ranger and Tonto protect an inheritance.

Sheriff Eli Parsons asks Hollister to help track down three convicts, one of them an innocent man. The chase leads Hollister and Claibourne into an Indian ambush. Hollister captures one of the fugitives and is able to identify the innocent man.

The mysterious Siesta Killer leaves a trail of blood and terror across the Southwest, striking during the siesta hours, when everyone's resting from the afternoon heat. Hollister foregoes a few of his cat naps to solve the mystery.
The true facts about Walter Scott, who became a flamboyant Western legend, that reveals the answer to a puzzle that baffled the nation for almost half a century.
A crystal ball sends the seventh daughter of a seventh daughter West to seek her fortune.

Victor Bers becomes bitter when his son, Hunt, deserts their farm to join the Confederate Army. They are suddenly reunited when the young soldier, attempting to evade a Union patrol, seeks refuge at the farm.
Bitter memories plague old Johnny Sunrise, one-time gunslinger. Though he is now crippled, Johnny plans a day of reckoning for a former antagonist.

Bill tries to find out who's been supplying whiskey to railroad workers during their working hours, thus slowing down their progress. He first suspects a lady saloon owner, but it soon becomes apparent she's not the one, and whoever it is is determined to stop the railroad from going through.

Bill's former Civil War commander asks him to take his oldest son with him on the trail to make a better man out of him. Bill agrees to this, but he detects that the son has an intense bitterness toward his father---until another man who served under his father in the war makes a claim the boy can't allow to go unchallenged.

program.episode_title: Bat Plays A Dead Man's Hand
program.synopsis: An unsophisticated miner runs afoul of a town boss and is framed for murder. When the town's lawyers refuse to represent him properly because they fear for their lives, Bat decides to represent the defendant in court himself.

When General Moran bets a spur of his railroad against a Texas horse ranch that a Kentucky thoroughbred can beat a Texas quarter-horse in a 440 yard sprint, he hires Bat to train his horse to win the wager.
An aspiring actress becomes the dupe of bandits.

When Hardie captures the notorious Johnny Ringo, the outlaw requests that he be able to visit his sister, who is dying. Ringo wishes for his criminal background to be kept secret, but another man may mess things up.

Hardie is sent to the town of Madden controlled by Effie Sutton when the Wells Fargo stages runs into problem on the line competing with Effie's own S&S stage line. The revolt by the people of Madden results in a murder and holdup.
In Medicine Bend, a crooked businessman has the town mayor and sheriff in his pocket while his henchmen raid the wagon trains passing through the region.
A doctor saves a local criminal from hanging by a mob, then tries to control his life.

On the run in the American Southwest, a boy encounters the infamous outlaw Billy the Kid and the legendary lawman Pat Garrett. He soon finds himself caught in the crossfire as Billy and Garrett square off in a deadly game of cat and mouse.
Bandits board a gold-carrying ship, murder most of the passengers and crew, and kidnap the captain's child.
A Spanish family claims ownership of a piece of land - but the deed can't be located.
A cavalry major makes peace with Indians despite an agitator working for carpetbaggers.
A half-breed gunslinger and a friend he hasn't seen in years join together to escort a shipment of explosives across Utah.
Lucas puts his life in jeopardy when he agrees to testify in a murder case where the first witness was killed before he could testify, and the killer is still at large.
Outlaw Sam Morley falls in love with a young woman who has a serious illness, and asks Lucas to turn him in for the reward and then use the money to pay for her medical care.
Outlaws control Reidsville, and the sheriff is powerless.
Gunmen pit homesteaders against cattlemen.

Hollister receives word that Kansas Joe Barton has been released from prison and is threatening to kill the judge who sentenced him who is now the Territorial Governor. Hollister puts into action a dangerous plan to lure the killer into the open.

Hollister captures outlaw Lafe Jackson who is on his way to a rendezvous with his gang to divide the proceeds of a robbery. Lafe kills both horses, forcing Clay to take his prisoner in on foot, giving Lafe's gang time to catch up and rescue him.
The story of Lt. Jessie Lee Reno, for whom the city of Reno was named.
Two prospectors claim they've discovered a fabulous diamond mine - which they must reluctantly sell.
Joel Begley, crippled in a mine accident, is now a bank teller. He is sitting in a restaurant across from the bank when the bank president is killed and the mine payroll taken.
Gil Durand, dishonorably discharged from the Army, is asked by the government to take the undercut position of locating a missing Army major.

Bill is brought by two bounty hunters to the Governor, who hires him as a marshal to clean up a border area which is being run by an outlaw known as El Sombro. Bill also learns that the sheriff of the main town in the area, his old mentor, has become a drunk and is totally ineffective in handling the situation.
After acting marshal Bill orders no guns to be worn on the streets of Rio Nada, a local casino owner sends for two notorious gunmen to kill him.
Bat decides to help a group of women campaigning for women's suffrage in Wyoming fight a cabal of wealthy ranchers who wish to prevent women from getting the vote.

program.episode_title: The Pied Piper Of Dodge City
program.synopsis: Bat imports Dolly McGregor to play the piano in Luke Short's saloon, but rival saloonkeeper, Mayor Webber, changes city ordinances to outlaw her. Bat gets the governor to set up a peace commission in order to get rid of Webber and clean up the town.

While riding shotgun to protect a valuable gold shipment, Hardie and his stagecoach driver have an interesting collection of passengers and cargo: a U.S. Army officer being sent to Fort Laramie to stand trial, a married couple and a baby.
Jim Hardie tries to prove a notorious gunslinger, Billy Reno, has been framed for murder and holding up the stagecoach after he realizes the case against Billy has come together far too easily.
During an attempted hold-up, Hardie learns the man he gunned down was an ex-convict who had gone straight after being released from prison and investigates him.

Hardie rides into Bridger to present a reward check to a man who helped prevent a stagecoach robbery. The fearful townspeople claim that they've never heard of the man and when the detective investigates, he discovers the hero's grave.
Stage coach passengers and outlaws travelling through Indian country are forced to join forces against the Apaches.
A gunfighter turns rancher at the mercy of a son who hates him and a gambler who wants to kill him.
A Chicago hotel clerk yearns for the Old West until he discovers, first hand, that the cowboy life is not what he'd imagined.

Brothers who battled on opposing sides of the Civil War return home to discover that their family has been kidnapped by a marauding band of rebel guerrillas who refuse to accept the defeat of the Confederacy.
A homely miner tries to win a pretty girl's heart.
A counterfeiter making a getaway joins a wagon train of Mormons headed for Salt Lake City.
Gold thieves and the government rile Geronimo, despite a cavalry captain's efforts at peace.
A Chicago hotel clerk yearns for the Old West until he discovers, first hand, that the cowboy life is not what he'd imagined.
Lucas welcomes his old friend Tom Birch, who once saved his life, unaware that the man is now a bank robber who is on the run with his gang.
Dan Maury, released from Yuma prison, arrives in North Fork intent on getting even with Marshal Torrance, who helped send him to jail.
The Lone Ranger and Tonto seek stage robbers.
A minister wants to build an orphanage on copper-bearing land.

While General Crook and Geronimo are negotiating a treaty, a fanatic brave challenges Geronimo's leadership and starts a series of bloody massacres in the territory. Hollister must devise a trap for the renegade to save Tombstone.

The Tombstone Epitaph's Editor Claibourne is following up on a report that a notorious outlaw is living in Tombstone as a respected citizen. Hollister thinks that this search is his jurisdiction and sets out to score a new beat on his good friend. The rivalry leads the two friends into grave danger when both find the answer to the mystery.
Culture comes to a mining town with the arrival of a piano, but the problem is finding someone who can play it.
A profiteer plans to capitalize on a Sacramento epidemic by selling snow to the pioneers.
Durant attempts to end ongoing negotiations with Cheyenne; Cullen and Elam scramble to prevent bloodshed in town.
Cullen must decide how much he is willing to risk; Durant faces financial ruin; Lily confronts the family of her late husband.
Cullen and Elam are on a mission of vengeance; Lily struggles to find her place.
Cullen and Lily work to meet the railroad's deadline; Elam and Joseph deal with the consequences of their violent mission.
Cullen must choose between dwelling on the past or to look forward to the future.
A Texas Ranger investigates a series of murders in an Old West town that's run by a former Confederate officer.
A Union deserter helps a group of women fight off a Comanche attack.
A gunslinger helps an ungrateful town fight off a raid by his former gang.
A war veteran takes on an evil arthritic rancher and his murderous daughter to stop them from forcing out settlers and laying claim to their land.
The Eagle enacts a plot to uncover the landowners behind the revolution.
Diego and Bernardo turn their hacienda into a haunted house.
Diego finds that The Eagle is stockpiling a deadly new weapon.
The Eagle begins gathering his army in an attempt to overthrow Los Angeles.
A woman's life is endangered after she testifies at a murder trial.
A Mexican bandit holds Dan Reid captive in a cave.
Sabotage and attempted murder play a part in the sale of a ranch.
An ex-convict is falsely accused of a bank robbery.
In Medicine Bend, a crooked businessman has the town mayor and sheriff in his pocket while his henchmen raid the wagon trains passing through the region.
Beautiful, biracial Pearl Chavez becomes the ward of her dead father's first love and finds herself torn between two brothers, one good and the other bad.
An Oregon ranchhand finds that his mountain cabin has been appropriated by a woman and her son.

A young boy is the only survivor after Crow Indians raid a wagon-train. He gets rescued by a group of Sioux Indians and the Chief renames the boy, raising him as his own. Once grown up he is conflicted between his tribe loyalty and white heritage.
An Army scout and an Indian chief resolve their differences in single combat.
Buffalo Bill and Wild Bill Hickock fight to establish the Pony Express despite Indians and California separatists who seek to destroy it.

Wanderer Rafe Covington swears an oath to his dying best friend to look after his beloved wife and Wyoming ranch, only to encounter other forces who strongly desire the ranch and the woman for their own purposes.
Having survived his own hanging, hired killer James Devlin turns over a new leaf, defending a widow and her son from a greedy land baron.
Chicago detectives in the 1880s head West to find the killers of their colleague.
Gold thieves and the government rile Geronimo, despite a cavalry captain's efforts at peace.
Raised since boyhood by a Pawnee chief, a young white man known as Pale Arrow finds his loyalty tested when he returns to his people and takes a job as a wagon train scout.
A cavalry major makes peace with Indians despite an agitator working for carpetbaggers.
A U.S. marshal and his helper cross Texas by wagon with four prisoners, one a dance-hall hostess.
A man enters a town and poses as a hangman, but he has intentions of freeing a gang of outlaws, including his brother, from the gallows.

Indian scout Tom Jeffords is sent out to stem the war between the Whites and Apaches in the late 1870s. He learns that the Indians kill only to protect themselves, or out of retaliation for white atrocities. Befriending the sagacious Apache leader Cochise, Jeffords ensures safe passage for white mail-carriers through Indian territory. As he becomes closer to the Native Americans, Jeffords falls in love with and weds a pretty Apache girl.
A bounty hunter trying to bring a murderer to justice is forced to accept the help of two less-than-trustworthy strangers.
A vicious outlaw kidnaps a sheriff and forces him to lead a motley group of adventurers to a cache of Apache gold.
Conflict arises between a noted, and aging, lawyer and a young associate who's defending a man accused of murder.

program.episode_title: Gold Is Where You Find It
program.synopsis: A detective disguises himself as a Mexican to clear himself of a robbery charge.
A Confederate soldier returns to his Arizona homestead and finds Union sympathizers have laid claim to his land.

Sheriff Chadbourne transports convict Ben Trask to El Paso in a covered wagon. The wagon also carries sea captain Theodore Bess and married couple Laura and Jerry Niblett. The group comes upon a wounded Native American, banished by his tribe, who offers to lead them to gold. But then the tribe attacks the wagon. Ben is freed to help, but the group's troubles have only begun.
Young Mark McCain is eager to help lawman Jay Jefferson, who has arrived in North Fork intent on killing an outlaw for the reward, whether he is the right man or not.
When escaped convicts hold up the general store in North Fork and take Marshal Torrance hostage, Lucas comes to the rescue.
The Lone Ranger goes after a scoundrel who is looking for gold.
An Easterner learns the hard way about the West.

His gun-arm badly wounded, Clay Hollister takes a vacation in Tucson where he encounters the brother of a killer he once brought to justice. With Clay at a disadvantage, he is forced to a shooting showdown left-handed.

Hollister doesn't know the identity of the brain directing an epidemic of outlawry in the Territory. But when handsome Horn Burnett and his brother commit murder, the attachment of a rancher's wife for the outlaw gives Clay a hint at the truth.

program.episode_title: The Man Who'd Bet On Anything
program.synopsis: The man who'd bet on anything has made a new wager: Carson City, Nevada, will someday be named the state capital.
The true story of a crusading reporter who investigates an Arizona land baron.
John Welker, a lawman embittered by the death of his fiancee, and Fitzgerald, a calculated bounty hunter, are both trailing a fleeing outlaw and his girl.

During the Mexican-American war in 1847, Captain Krag has a wide reputation for bravery. He and Corporal Durbin are taken prisoner by the enemy and face severe interrogation and torture. Krag pleads with Durbin not to submit to the enemy.

Longley has been a special marshal to the Texas town of Rio Nada. The area has been plagued by Mexican bandits who have used to the cross the border into the United States and raid the countryside. The gang is led by a mysterious leader known only as "El Sombro". When Longley captures an agent for El Sombro, he hopes to use the threat of hanging for the attempted murder of a deputy sheriff to force him to identify the illusive bandit chief.
Bill is forced to kill one of the Dawson brothers after a fight when the man draws on him, and then he must contend with three other brothers who vow to kill him.

When Hugh Blaine bets $50,000 that all four hooves of a racehorse are off the ground at once, he agrees to pay Bat 10% of the bet to supply proof. Bat hires a man to take photographs of the trotter in action, but the pictures are stolen.

Bat and his partners fleece an unscrupulous businessman out of half the profits in a gold mine. He exacts revenge by buying up the timber they need to shore up the mine walls, then tries to kill the men with an explosion in the mine.
Two sideshow hypnotists use their powers to fleece a gold-mine owner.

Jim's train is robbed and he is left without funds in a small town whose businessmen refuse to grant him credit. With the help of a friendly old pickpocket, Hardie manages to alleviate his money problems and bring the thieves to justice.
A local sheriff suspects their new teacher, an ex-convict, is the murderer of a Wells Fargo agent, however Hardie is convinced that the killer is one of the town’s leading citizens.
A former bank robber searches for his double-crossing partner who left him for dead, while having to look after a 6-year-old girl.
Notorious gunfighter Jimmy Ringo rides into town to find his true love, who doesn't want to see him. He hasn't come looking for trouble, but trouble finds him around every corner.
Beautiful, biracial Pearl Chavez becomes the ward of her dead father's first love and finds herself torn between two brothers, one good and the other bad.
A New England family find life in a gold camp harder than they'd anticipated.
A prospector, double-crossed by his partner and left to die in the desert, is saved by an Indian girl.
Raised since boyhood by a Pawnee chief, a young white man known as Pale Arrow finds his loyalty tested when he returns to his people and takes a job as a wagon train scout.
A Chicago hotel clerk yearns for the Old West until he discovers, first hand, that the cowboy life is not what he'd imagined.

Young Mark manages to capture a hawk, but then falls into danger from a rattlesnake. When a stranger comes to his rescue, the grateful Mark invites the man home, unaware that he is on the run from the law.
Young Mark McCain is eager to help lawman Jay Jefferson, who has arrived in North Fork intent on killing an outlaw for the reward, whether he is the right man or not.
The ranger poses as an outlaw to foil a vicious gang.
Two town bankers plan to swindle the citizens.

Sheriff Clay Hollister goes undercover as prisoner Bert Taggert in order to prevent Mexican bandit chief Juan Diega from robbing the Tombstone bank in order to finance his revolutionaries. But then the real Taggert shows up.

Sheriff Hollister must collect taxes from a nest of outlaws who are occupying different hideouts far from Tombstone but within the county. It's a mission he views as leading to an almost certain death.
Meek Stubby Weeks and brawny Dutch Hogan compete for the same girl.
A couple on the verge of divorce is held hostage by a killer.
Leona Butler, a frontier mother, is overcome with grief when her son is accidentally killed by an Indian. Believing that the death was not accidental, Leona refuses to forgive the Indians.

Watson Cooke, a New York newspaper reporter, comes to the West to do a story on Sheriff Agate Slade. The reporter forms an immediate dislike for the sheriff and he sets out to ruin Slade's reputation.

After Bill, again working with the railroad, turns down a bribe from a landowner to detour the track through his town, the man then forces a doctor to quarantine the railroad workers, falsely claiming a case of cholera.

Bill finds an old prospector almost dead from thirst, and brings him in to the railroad workers camp. But men from a rival outfit talk the old man into spreading a story about finding gold, hoping to lure the workers away from their job.

A stagecoach carrying Bat and Claire, the local sheriff's fiancée, is robbed and Claire is kidnapped. With the sheriff wounded and out of action, Bat tries to rescue Claire and capture the entire gang of kidnappers by himself.

When two speculators try to corner the stock market by sending former Pony Express riders racing from the mining towns to Denver with the latest information, Bat helps his friend counter the move with by sending news by carrier pigeon.
Two vengeful brothers hold their former jailer captive in a barbed wire enclosure.
When a Wells Fargo manager notices the new guard wears a belt buckle similar to those worn by a notorious family of Texas outlaws, he begins to have second thoughts of his new hire.

Jim investigates the murder of a Wells Fargo express agent. His only lead is a young wounded outlaw. With the help of the local sheriff, Jim pretends to be Jesse James and tricks the youthful criminal into taking him to the gang's hideout.
When an Army scout retires to a farm in New Mexico, he takes pity on a white woman and her half-breed son recently rescued from Indians, and invites them to join him.
A man is chasing four outlaws who killed his wife and finds them in a small town's jail, but they escape to Mexico.
A doctor saves a local criminal from hanging by a mob, then tries to control his life.
An Army private tries to save an emaciated racehorse that is about to be destroyed.
It's Bill Bottle's birthday and anyone bearing the name of Bottle has been invited to a party.
Gold thieves and the government rile Geronimo, despite a cavalry captain's efforts at peace.
A gunfighter turns rancher at the mercy of a son who hates him and a gambler who wants to kill him.

The McCains try to be tolerant of their embittered neighbor Abel MacDonald, who tells Lucas to keep off his land and won't allow his son to be friends with Mark, but he goes too far when he won't allow his injured boy to get medical treatment.
An unfounded rumor causes North Fork's outspoken feminist schoolteacher to be attacked by a mob of angry townspeople.
Dan Reid must turn over his horse to rustlers to help the Lone Ranger.
Tonto and the Lone Ranger set out to stop a planned Indian uprising.

After an outlaw escapes from a stagecoach killing, Editor Claibourne spearheads a drive in Tombstone to raise a $5,000 reward for his capture, but this may cause problems for Sheriff Hollister who must also now deal with greedy bounty hunters.
After Sam Brewster's son gets shot in self defense by Deputy Charlie, only Curly Bill Brocius decides to stand with the sheriff when they meet Brewster's men at the outskirts of town.
A preacher finds the going rough in a rugged desert town.
There's trouble on the honeymoon when a newlywed falls for a phony gold-mine swindle.
Embittered and lonely, gunman Clint Shannon rides into Del Rio to see his nephew, Frank. Shannon finds the young man under the thumb of scheming Sally Esky.

Jared Fletcher, a Union Army purchasing agent, has been given money to obtain supplies for Rebel prisoners. But he intends to keep the money for himself and he orders his wife not to reveal his intentions.

A group of convicts take over the work train, killing their guards, and hold the entire crew at the construction site hostage. Bill, who they think they also killed on the train, makes it back to the site and must figure out a way to stop them.

With his job about done, Bill is planning to leave the railroad workers camp and move on, until a former crewman once fired by MacMorris shows up to make trouble, and Bill learns the man is working as field agent for the company that the workers hoped to get a new contract from.

Bat's friend, Ellie Winters, is hired by promoters selling shares in their diamond mine. Although Ellie is convinced that the mine is legitimate, Bat investigates and discovers the owners are salting the mine with high-grade quartz.
On a seemingly routine trip to investigate a shortage in a small town bank, Bat Masterson finds a dead man, makes the acquaintance of a larcenous dog, and is very nearly killed himself.
Two bandits flee to Mexico with Hardie hot on their trail.
Hardie witnesses the murder of a Wells Fargo agent and quickly focuses on several suspects, including two employees who have dark secrets they wish to keep hidden.

When a stage breaks down, a passenger who is returning home after three years in prison for a crime he said he didn't commit, becomes upset with his treatment. Hardie decides to take a gamble on him with a loan to solve a dispute.
A half-breed gunslinger and a friend he hasn't seen in years join together to escort a shipment of explosives across Utah.

Sheriff Chadbourne transports convict Ben Trask to El Paso in a covered wagon. The wagon also carries sea captain Theodore Bess and married couple Laura and Jerry Niblett. The group comes upon a wounded Native American, banished by his tribe, who offers to lead them to gold. But then the tribe attacks the wagon. Ben is freed to help, but the group's troubles have only begun.
A judge attempts to bring a killer to justice in a small wild west town. Unfortunately the town is owned by the suspected killer's father.
Notorious gunfighter Jimmy Ringo rides into town to find his true love, who doesn't want to see him. He hasn't come looking for trouble, but trouble finds him around every corner.
An Army scout and an Indian chief resolve their differences in single combat.
A Texas Ranger investigates a series of murders in an Old West town that's run by a former Confederate officer.
When his employer begins to suspect him of embezzlement, bookkeeper Asa Manning decides to hire a professional gunman to take care of the problem.

Although a drought has forced local cowhands to seek work on a railroad construction project, railraod executives are afraid that their workers will desert them if they believe an old man's claim to have found a way to make rain.
The Lone Ranger and Tonto teach a lesson to their young friend Tommy.
A gang of desperadoes terrorizes Bakersville and the surrounding countryside.

After killing a man in self defense in the town of Galeno, Sheriff Hollister finds himself hauled before a kangaroo court for trial on a murder charge, the penalty of which is death. An unexpected ally comes to Hollister's defense.

Raoul de Moreney, a slick card shark and fortune hunter, comes to Tombstone in search of quick riches. His vicious greed nettles the editor of the Epitaph and in a flush of anger, the two men agree to a duel... but de Moreney is a deadly duelist.

program.episode_title: The Longest Beard In The World
program.synopsis: A Congressional candidate is urged to go to the Chicago World's Fair - as the man with the longest beard in the world.
A San Francisco merchant, hearing that China's about to ban the export of rice, proceeds to corner the rice market.

Loy Bannister is the town's most powerful figure because of his political and economic influence. In a wild bid to gain Bannister's admiration, young Branch Neeley agrees to stand trial for a murder he didn't commit.
The Army wants to get an Indian chief, Alou, to sign a peace treaty. Young Corporal Smith is given command of a cavalry unit and ordered to bring the Indian to the fort.

Bill saves a government agent from ambush. The agent is carrying a list of landowners who are refusing to pay their taxes, and the daughter of one of those landowners---the one who the attempted killers are working for---is engaged to the governor.

Bill finds a town engulfed by a feud between two ranchers. He also learns that his former commanding officer, now a doctor, has turned to drinking, and that a young man blames him for crippling his arm. Bill tries to talk the doctor into staying and not running from the challenges.
Bat Masterson finds himself doing a most unwelcome "land office business." Accepting a parcel of wasteland from Lisa Truex in payment for a debt, he decides to turn it into a grave yard.

Bat receives half of a cane he gave to Jane Taylor and rides to her ranch to investigate. He is forced to choose sides between her family and other ranchers and his old friend, a cattle baron intent on returning to the days of open range.

Bob Dawson has been paroled to Jim Hardie and is living at Brisbane, Nebraska where he regales the people with stories he is writing about them. Worried about his stories someone hires Nedy West to kill him and steal his manuscript.

A Choctaw boy shows up with a note from Wells Fargo talking about a boy being shipped to his parents from his grandmother. Hardie must determine if he is the boy in the note and where his parents are.

While attempting to deliver a golden owl to its wealthy purchaser, Hardie is attacked and the owl is replaced by one of lead. Meanwhile, a Chinese religious society claims the owl was stolen from their temple in China.
After being gunned down by Johnny Reno, Jim Hardie is promoted to District Superintendent but finds he doesn't like office work. He decides to join his replacement to go after Reno.
After a quiet drifter is met with violence and bullying in a small town, he finds himself in a vengeful struggle with the local marshal.

Karl Westover, an inexperienced farm boy, runs away after unintentionally killing a neighbor, whose family pursues him for vengeance. He meets Barbarosa, a gunman of near-mythical proportions, who is himself in danger from his father-in-law.

Revisionist western about fallen preacher Shay, who guns down his wife Raysha for running off with another man. Wandering, he meets single mom Laurie. However, helpless sheriff Scoby wants Shay to help him fight the villainous Clavers.
An Oregon ranchhand finds that his mountain cabin has been appropriated by a woman and her son.

Monte Walsh is a cowboy in 1890s Wyoming, who finds calls for his expertise waning, as civilisation advances west. Should he head out for the last remaining wide open spaces, or stay with his sweetheart and take a job with the travelling Wild West show run by Colonel Wilson?
Lucas McCain gives blood to save a dying man and is surprised by Marshal Torrance's odd behavior toward the man. Then three men arrive from a nearby town and demand custody of the stranger.
Mark's finding of the body of a murder victim starts Lucas, his friend Artemus, and Marshal Torrance on a search for the killer.
A cattle baron's plan for becoming king of the Arizona territory involves murder.
The friends find escaped convicts and treasure.

Gunslinger Jake Hoyt plots a radical means of rising to power in Tombstone by snatching the sheriff's job away from Clay Hollister. To succeed, Hoyt plans to outshine Hollister as a sharpshooter. The result is inevitable: a dramatic showdown.

While the survivors of an Indian attack dig the victim's grave, they discover a vein of silver. The strike ignites a wrathful dispute between the two prospectors and the dead man's kinfolk over shares of ownership of the new-found fortune.
A woman isn't too happy about the attentions of a drunk - but she's even less happy when Faro Bill kills the man.
The beginnings of the Mexican War.
Cullen and Elam are on opposite sides of the law; Durant and Lily push the railroad west.
Durant confronts a threat to Hell on Wheels; a crime forces Lily and Elam to strike a deal.
Mickey and Sean's lives hang in the balance; Cullen tries to keep order; Lily and Elam work together to clean up a mess.
Cullen has to diffuse an internal threat to the railroad; tragedy strikes.
Cullen is haunted by his past; the fate of the railroad hangs in the balance.

On the run in the American Southwest, a boy encounters the infamous outlaw Billy the Kid and the legendary lawman Pat Garrett. He soon finds himself caught in the crossfire as Billy and Garrett square off in a deadly game of cat and mouse.
In Medicine Bend, a crooked businessman has the town mayor and sheriff in his pocket while his henchmen raid the wagon trains passing through the region.
A runaway teenager and a horse breeder strike an unlikely friendship in the context of common hardships.
Diego investigates a string of robberies in Monterey.
Diego sets a trap to reveal who is behind the Monterey robberies.
Diego hands over his investment to Verdugo but a thief still lurks.
Pablo demands 45,000 pesos in exchange for Verdugo's life.
When a newspaper opposes him, a crooked politician steals the press.
A young widow is harrassed by the gang that killed her husband.
An old pioneer nearly loses his life when three escaped killers visit him.
Tonto and the Lone Ranger search for a white man who's responsible for murders and Indian uprisings.
Stage coach passengers and outlaws travelling through Indian country are forced to join forces against the Apaches.
Having survived his own hanging, hired killer James Devlin turns over a new leaf, defending a widow and her son from a greedy land baron.

Karl Westover, an inexperienced farm boy, runs away after unintentionally killing a neighbor, whose family pursues him for vengeance. He meets Barbarosa, a gunman of near-mythical proportions, who is himself in danger from his father-in-law.
A cynical former sheriff turned bounty hunter helps a young, recently appointed acting sheriff with his advice, his experience and his gun.
An unemployed cowhand is hired by his dishonest friend as muscle in a dispute between homesteaders and a cattleman.
After serving a prison sentence, a farmer returns to his Pacific Northwest gold rush town to retrieve his adolescent son who was left in the care of a dance hall singer.

program.episode_title: Good Guys And The Bad Guys, The
program.synopsis: An aging lawman and an aging outlaw join forces when their respective positions in society are usurped by a younger but incompetent Marshal, and a younger but vicious gang leader.

Mountain man and fur trapper Flint Mitchell is planning a hunting trek into the beaver-rich Blackhawk Territory, despite protests from his Indian friend who tells him that the land belongs to Indians.
During California's gold-rush, an undercover marshal, posing as a gunfighter, investigates the murder of another marshal who had been assigned to the region.
The kidnappers demand Anna Maria meet them at the ruined mission.
A young man is sentenced to a public flogging by the acting governor.
During California's gold-rush, an undercover marshal, posing as a gunfighter, investigates the murder of another marshal who had been assigned to the region.
A doctor saves a local criminal from hanging by a mob, then tries to control his life.

Monte Walsh is a cowboy in 1890s Wyoming, who finds calls for his expertise waning, as civilisation advances west. Should he head out for the last remaining wide open spaces, or stay with his sweetheart and take a job with the travelling Wild West show run by Colonel Wilson?
A Confederate soldier returns to his Arizona homestead and finds Union sympathizers have laid claim to his land.

Wanderer Rafe Covington swears an oath to his dying best friend to look after his beloved wife and Wyoming ranch, only to encounter other forces who strongly desire the ranch and the woman for their own purposes.

Brothers who battled on opposing sides of the Civil War return home to discover that their family has been kidnapped by a marauding band of rebel guerrillas who refuse to accept the defeat of the Confederacy.
The Sackett brothers seek their fortunes out West after the Civil War.
The Sackett brothers seek their fortunes out West after the Civil War.
When an Army scout retires to a farm in New Mexico, he takes pity on a white woman and her half-breed son recently rescued from Indians, and invites them to join him.
Sheriff Pat Garrett's assignment: bring in Billy the Kid - who's just refused a pardon.
A man entrusts his money to a pal for safekeeping. Soon after, the friend dies - and the cash is nowhere to be found.
A champion logroller is as bashful as he is brawny: he shuns the advances of a lovely lady.
A dancehall queen is hampering the railroad's efficiency.
Beautiful, biracial Pearl Chavez becomes the ward of her dead father's first love and finds herself torn between two brothers, one good and the other bad.
When renegade Apaches wound Lucas and kidnap Mark, Marshal Torrance joins with U.S. Marshal Sam Buckhart to form a rescue posse.

Lucas is the town's representative to the railroad commission until an infamous train robber dies and leaves his ill-gotten wealth to Lucas. Lucas must clear his name and stop the reckless ambition of his replacement.
A misguided youth learns values.
Aunt Maggie sets fire to a shack where the Ranger and Tonto are prisoners.
Hollister is forced to lead an outlaw and his partners across the desert after they overcome him.

An enraged Apache brave kills the white man who murdered his father. Both Sheriff Hollister and the victim's brother pursue the Indian with very different goals and trap him in a box canyon that the Apache has rigged with dynamite.
Alex Todd's mail enterprise is doing pretty well, a condition his competition is about to end. Starring Clint Eastwood.
Author Bret Harte arrives in the West, where the colorful residents inspire his story "The Outcasts of Poker Flat."
Marshal Ben Clark is accustomed to the danger and excitement of the Western frontier. Settling in a small Kansas town, he finds it hard to adjust.

Cattle baron Dave King, who has tremendous land holdings, is suddenly faced with the loss of everything he owns when the bottom drops out of the cattle market. Citizens who have resented the King's success are elated over his misfortune.

A lynch mob wants to hang a young man convicted of killing a family of homesteaders. Bill rides in with a stay of execution from the governor just as the mob is about to break into the jail and the desperate prisoner escapes.

Bill stops to visit his friend, a well-respected sheriff, only to find that the man has been jailed for killing an unarmed youth, and the whole town has turned against him, including his own son. Bill seeks to find the real truth even though the sheriff himself tries to discourage him.

Three cowboys trick a magician into helping them rob a bank by taking Bat Masterson's gun "as a joke" before he enters the bank. Bat and the magician team up to recover the loot through the aid of another conjurer's stunt.

Bat travels to Yuma to give away the bride at a wedding but discovers the girl's murdered fiancée on the way there, and he joins the posse sent to bring the suspected killer, the Yaqui Kid, to justice.
A shady banker attempts the perfect crime – the theft of $100,000 from his own bank.

When Butch Cassidy is released from prison, Hardie is tasked with asking him to work for Wells Fargo. While on a train, Butch meets an outlaw friend, Idaho, who with others are planning to rob the train.
Hardie arrives to negotiate with the Pawnees who have killed a crew's hunter so that a stagecoach road through the Dakota Black Hills can be completed.
An Army scout and an Indian chief resolve their differences in single combat.

A young boy is the only survivor after Crow Indians raid a wagon-train. He gets rescued by a group of Sioux Indians and the Chief renames the boy, raising him as his own. Once grown up he is conflicted between his tribe loyalty and white heritage.
After a quiet drifter is met with violence and bullying in a small town, he finds himself in a vengeful struggle with the local marshal.
The story of Harvard's venerable Washington elm.
A man must transport 500 mail-order brides from Boston to the state of Washington.
In 1846, American forces move northward, leaving an inviting target to the Mexicans - sparsely manned Los Angeles.
Bandit Red Haskell baffles lawmen by using his double to provide him with alibis.
A vicious outlaw kidnaps a sheriff and forces him to lead a motley group of adventurers to a cache of Apache gold.
When a rancher dies after the bank forecloses the mortgage on his property, his son blames the banker for his father's death.
When a wealthy rancher is killed, Mark suggests that they seek the aid of a mind reader to identify the murderer.
The Lone Ranger and Tonto go after an unscrupulous lawyer who has been forging documents.
The Lone Ranger stops a runaway stagecoach.

Gunman Nate Crandall murders a rancher to steal $20,000. Sheriff Hollister gets to the loot first and locks it in a safe. Determined to get the money, Crandall involves young Jim Edwards in a plot to rob the safe and take the fall for the killing.

Reverend Tuttle is aboard the stagecoach when it is robbed by three masked gunmen. Sheriff Hollister suspects Big Jim Sten, boss of a nearby town, is behind the robbery. But when the reverend and Hollister visit Sten's town, a secret is revealed.
Tombstone, Arizona, is in for it: a dauntless woman has decided it's time for reform.
Dr. Edward Wilson attempts to curb an epidemic raging in a Paiute village.

Stella Faring, widowed by the Civil War, is anxious to regain the house she was forced to abandon during the war. She finds that a stranger, Cass Taggart, purchased the house from the Federal Government and is living in it.
Two Confederate soldiers are sent into the woods on a reconnaissance mission. They suddenly discover that a Yankee has spotted them.
Bill comes to the defense of an unassuming man who is being bullied and tries to encourage him to stand up for himself. Soon the man asks Bill to teach him how to use a gun.
Bill takes the job of escorting a convicted killer to his hanging, knowing that the man's sons and gang members are in the area planning to free him.

While awaiting a murder trail in which he will be the star witness, Bat visits a Mexican ranch where, he is told, a fortune in jewels awaits. It's a trap, though, set by the defendant's uncle and Bat will face a firing squad at dawn.
When Ellie Winters is framed for murder, she desperately telegraphs Bat for help. He bails her out to help search for a missing witness and enlists Wyatt Earp to handle her case.
Ex-Confederate soldiers devise a master plan to ruin the government, involving stealing a money-filled casket.
Hardie arrives in Medicine River to help set up a new office but the locals are unhappy that he is the driver of the route.

Jim Hardie's task of catching the Manning Brothers is interrupted when his attempt to help a stranded man backfires. The man, John Leslie Nagel, has a $25,000 bounty on him so he trusts no one, including Hardie, who has no interest in him.
Buffalo Bill and Wild Bill Hickock fight to establish the Pony Express despite Indians and California separatists who seek to destroy it.
An Oregon ranchhand finds that his mountain cabin has been appropriated by a woman and her son.
A U.S. marshal and his helper cross Texas by wagon with four prisoners, one a dance-hall hostess.
Mary Granger is the focal point of a quarrel between two miners: one is in love with her, the other's out to get her father's property.
A gunslinger helps an ungrateful town fight off a raid by his former gang.

Revisionist western about fallen preacher Shay, who guns down his wife Raysha for running off with another man. Wandering, he meets single mom Laurie. However, helpless sheriff Scoby wants Shay to help him fight the villainous Clavers.
A group of outlaws devise a plan to get rid of Lucas so that they can take over the town of North Fork.
After Lucas kills a mountain man in a barrom brawl, his father arrives in North Fork determined to take revenge on Lucas and Marshal Torrance, once he breaks his other two boys out of jail.
A country doctor helps track an outlaw band.
A bank robber poses as the Lone Ranger.

Jett Sergeant, son of a notorious outlaw, sees Hollister kill his father in a shootout and vows he will kill the Sheriff. Clay tries to set him straight, but the youth teams up with two of his father's henchmen in a plot to murder the Sheriff.

Recently released from prison, Frank Leslie returns to Tombstone to marry the widow of the man he killed and pledges never to use guns again. But four brothers of Leslie's victim come to Tombstone intent on revenge, putting his oath to the test.
Outlaw Belle Clayton and her gang are more determined than ever to rob a Montana-bound train.

program.episode_title: The Man Who Was Never Licked
program.synopsis: Elias Jackson Baldwin, caught up in the gold frenzy, heads for California in 1853 to seek his fortune.
Released after a long prison term, prospector Goad is ready to resume panning for gold, and plans to take his son with him.

Marshal Monty Elstrode is captured by an outlaw gang planning to organize a new Confederate Army. Elstrode is shocked to learn that the leader of the group is the supposedly dead murderer of President Lincoln, John Wilkes Booth.

An Easterner and his daughter hire Bill to guide them in the search for a wild stallion whom they hope to breed. But an old enemy plans to follow them on the search and rob the horse breeder and get revenge on Bill.
Bill goes after three men who forced him to accompany them on a bank robbery and framed him for it.

Boxing-Fan Bat Masterson, arrested en-route to a championship bout, finds himself arraigned before infamous "fining judge," Malachi Brody. The judge is up for reelection, and Bat has an idea to take advantage of it.

After Bat and the stagecoach he is riding is robbed, Bat's plan to catch the gang of stagecoach robbers backfires, when not only the silver bullion the stagecoach was carrying is stolen, but the stagecoach itself disappears.
Hardie is marked for murder by a vengeful man he sent to prison.

Jim Hardie is sent by Wells Fargo to Comanche, Texas to find the Rucker brothers. Unknown to Hardie, John Wesley Hardin, a cousin to the Rucker brothers, is there and still holding a grudge against Hardie.

Hardie is sent to confer with the Colonel at Fort Chaplin which is closing now that a peace treaty has been signed. Wells Fargo is concerned about a rash of robberies in the area by a gang led by an ex-Army officer who deserted the Army.
A Texas Ranger investigates a series of murders in an Old West town that's run by a former Confederate officer.

On the run in the American Southwest, a boy encounters the infamous outlaw Billy the Kid and the legendary lawman Pat Garrett. He soon finds himself caught in the crossfire as Billy and Garrett square off in a deadly game of cat and mouse.

An injured cowboy on the run falls for the young daughter of a Quaker family that takes him in. As his violent past starts to catch up to him, he is forced to choose between his former life or a peaceful future.
A cynical former sheriff turned bounty hunter helps a young, recently appointed acting sheriff with his advice, his experience and his gun.
A man enters a town and poses as a hangman, but he has intentions of freeing a gang of outlaws, including his brother, from the gallows.
Newly arrived gunslinger Al Walker throws a mammoth party at the saloon, keeping acting Marshal Ben Waller busy until Lucas is forced to intervene.
Newspaperman Byron Claremont specializes in profiles of famous gunmen. One of his stories was responsible for an old-time marshal's death; and his next subject is Lucas.
A mob blames a freight-line owner for a gold robbery.
The Lone Ranger and Tonto avenge wrongs throughout the Old West.

Hollister and Claibourne journey to a neighboring town and find it evacuated in anticipation of the arrival of an outlaw band. Clay and the editor are captured and the leader of the marauders is killed as a new outlaw takes command.

Sheriff Hollister has to fire a young deputy for being "trigger happy." The youngster takes a job as guard in the Saloon, where he unnecessarily guns down a popular rancher. When Hollister orders him out of town, the young man defies him.
An Army-Navy rivalry complicates the delivery of gold from California to Washington, D.C.
In 1847, Lt. Edward Beale tries to promote the use of camels in the U.S.'s southwestern deserts.
Jenny Breckenridge is accompanying her meek husband, Harlan, on a journey to the West. At an outpost, a stranger threatens them.
Mace, a cattleman, hates sheepherders because he believes that sheep ruin good grazing land. He takes a liking to a young man and sets out to train him as a good cattleman.
Bill befriends an overeager young man named Johnny Tuvo who plans to challenge him in the upcoming horse race. But two other men at the event have more sinister plans for how to take advantage of it.

Bill is captured by a posse and accused of being one of the men who robbed the bank and shot the banker. Unfortunately, the victim claims Bill was the robber who shot him. Only the sheriff, whom the banker hates for being in love with his daughter, is inclined to believe Bill.
A horse thief hijacks a herd of cavalry stock from horse trader Bat Masterson. Bat's problem - find and deliver the horses - and bring the criminal to justice.

Bat must clear his name when underworld character Mace Gunnison takes over the town of Liberal, Kansas, appropriates Bat Masterson's name and identity, and fleeces townsmen and trail-hands at crooked games of chance.
Jim Hardie is accompanying a stagecoach carrying a Senator because three ranchers have hired a gunman to kill the Senator to protect their land.
When Wells Fargo is threatened with bankruptcy if they don't find the source of counterfeit Bills of Exchange used to move money, Hardie is forced to find the source himself.

Hardie teams up with railroad investigator, Mike Forbes, to solve a robbery of their joint office in Amity, Kansas. As they solve the robbery a second robbery occurs involving a murder and a stabbing causing issues with some Texas cowboys.
Jack Kramer, soon to be executed, asks Hardie to help his son change his evil tendency in exchange for stolen Wells Fargo gold. The plan goes left when Kramer’s murderous partner follows.
A Confederate soldier returns to his Arizona homestead and finds Union sympathizers have laid claim to his land.

Brothers who battled on opposing sides of the Civil War return home to discover that their family has been kidnapped by a marauding band of rebel guerrillas who refuse to accept the defeat of the Confederacy.

Larry, Moe, and Curly Joe work for an editor at a Boston wildlife conservation magazine and make such a mess of the pressroom that their publisher gets rid of them by sending them out west to stop the slaughter of buffalo. Upon their arrival they find themselves being sought after by every notorious gunslinger in history. Luckily, pistol-packing Annie Oakley, who has fallen in love with the handsome editor, agrees to protect them against the bad guys.

program.episode_title: Good Guys And The Bad Guys, The
program.synopsis: An aging lawman and an aging outlaw join forces when their respective positions in society are usurped by a younger but incompetent Marshal, and a younger but vicious gang leader.
A Texas Ranger investigates a series of murders in an Old West town that's run by a former Confederate officer.

On the run in the American Southwest, a boy encounters the infamous outlaw Billy the Kid and the legendary lawman Pat Garrett. He soon finds himself caught in the crossfire as Billy and Garrett square off in a deadly game of cat and mouse.
Gavin and Ezra Martin think that Lucas killed their son Clemmie while taking part in a train robbery, and Lucas refuses to tell them what really happened.
After the daughter of Lucas's friend the hotel clerk arrives in North Fork, two strangers threaten her life.

program.episode_title: The Woman In The White Mask
program.synopsis: A woman seeks revenge for an old injustice.
A bounty hunter plans to turn in an outlaw, then help him escape.

Augustine Ramirez, has been crossing the border into Tombstone Territory to prey on the ranchers and then retreat to Mexico, where international law protects him. Hollister goes undercover to infiltrate the bandit's lair.

Hollister learns that the hatchet men of a powerful Chinese society are on a mission to find a society member's girlfriend who has been hidden away by a Chinese Quarter resident. Hollister faces a type of danger unfamiliar to the West.
California's first schoolmarm has her class constantly interrupted by a bear.
Only two people believe that a bank teller accused of embezzling is innocent - everyone else is ready to lynch him.
Durant fights for his life and the railroad; Cullen tries to save an old friend.
Lily and Cullen struggle to maintain order; Elam plans for the future.
A new arrival brings chaos; Eva is caught between Elam and Mr. Toole.
Cullen is interrogated about the destruction of Hell on Wheels.
Cullen and Lily take over the railroad and the Sioux attack; Eva attends Toole's funeral.

Monte Walsh is a cowboy in 1890s Wyoming, who finds calls for his expertise waning, as civilisation advances west. Should he head out for the last remaining wide open spaces, or stay with his sweetheart and take a job with the travelling Wild West show run by Colonel Wilson?

Wanderer Rafe Covington swears an oath to his dying best friend to look after his beloved wife and Wyoming ranch, only to encounter other forces who strongly desire the ranch and the woman for their own purposes.
A former bank robber searches for his double-crossing partner who left him for dead, while having to look after a 6-year-old girl.
Joaquin demands vengeance when the Especials kill one of his friends.

program.episode_title: Zorro And The Flag Of Truce
program.synopsis: Diego negotiates a truce between Joaquin and the returning governor.
Joaquin targets the governor after the failed truce.
A friend of Diego's sees him as competition for Anna Maria's hand.
A tenderfoot tries to replace his murdered lawman brother.
A retired outlaw offers shelter to a pair of bank robbers until he sees the influence they have on his grandson.
A hypnotist steals Hardrock Hazen's gold.
Ruthless Hutch Conent controls new sheriff Jack Morrison's town.
An unemployed cowhand is hired by his dishonest friend as muscle in a dispute between homesteaders and a cattleman.
The Sackett brothers seek their fortunes out West after the Civil War.
The Sackett brothers seek their fortunes out West after the Civil War.
A judge attempts to bring a killer to justice in a small wild west town. Unfortunately the town is owned by the suspected killer's father.
Stage coach passengers and outlaws travelling through Indian country are forced to join forces against the Apaches.
A Union deserter helps a group of women fight off a Comanche attack.
A gunslinger helps an ungrateful town fight off a raid by his former gang.
A U.S. marshal and his helper cross Texas by wagon with four prisoners, one a dance-hall hostess.
Raised since boyhood by a Pawnee chief, a young white man known as Pale Arrow finds his loyalty tested when he returns to his people and takes a job as a wagon train scout.
A former confederate soldier journeys to the transcontinental railroad to seek vengeance.
Cullen fights for his life as he answers for his actions; Lily struggles to survive in the wilderness; Durant attempts to spin tragedy for political gain.
Cullen is diverted in the search of the sergeant; Durant organizes a search party to find Lily Bell.
Cullen closes in on the sergeant; Durant has a hidden agenda while helping Lily recuperate.
Durant's answer to the railroad's financial problems spurs a brawl between Cullen and Elam.
Durant attempts to end ongoing negotiations with Cheyenne; Cullen and Elam scramble to prevent bloodshed in town.
Cullen must decide how much he is willing to risk; Durant faces financial ruin; Lily confronts the family of her late husband.
Cullen and Elam are on a mission of vengeance; Lily struggles to find her place.
Cullen and Lily work to meet the railroad's deadline; Elam and Joseph deal with the consequences of their violent mission.
Cullen must choose between dwelling on the past or to look forward to the future.
A Texas Ranger investigates a series of murders in an Old West town that's run by a former Confederate officer.

On the run in the American Southwest, a boy encounters the infamous outlaw Billy the Kid and the legendary lawman Pat Garrett. He soon finds himself caught in the crossfire as Billy and Garrett square off in a deadly game of cat and mouse.
After a quiet drifter is met with violence and bullying in a small town, he finds himself in a vengeful struggle with the local marshal.
The Sackett brothers seek their fortunes out West after the Civil War.
